## Entry 119
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T18:20:12.084Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:20:12.084Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_ee1maIdZQQfFbxJiEz9oBifB",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 4b24a0\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 723\nOutput:\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nimport re\n\nFLOOD_KEYWORDS = {\n    \"flood\",\n    \"rain\",\n    \"inundation\",\n    \"drainage\",\n    \"lake\",\n    \"waterlogging\",\n    \"barricade\",\n}\nHEAT_KEYWORDS = {\n    \"aqi\",\n    \"air quality\",\n    \"heat\",\n    \"uhi\",\n    \"pollution\",\n    \"pm2.5\",\n    \"pm10\",\n    \"no2\",\n}\nINFRA_KEYWORDS = {\n    \"power\",\n    \"outage\",\n    \"grid\",\n    \"bescom\",\n    \"tree fall\",\n    \"wind\",\n}\nLOGISTICS_KEYWORDS = {\n    \"traffic\",\n    \"corridor\",\n    \"delay\",\n    \"route\",\n    \"logistics\",\n    \"orr\",\n    \"hosur\",\n}\nSCORECARD_KEYWORDS = {\"risk\", \"scorecard\", \"vulnerability\", \"assessment\", \"how vulnerable is\"}\nGREETING_PHRASES = {\n    \"hi\",\n    \"hello\",\n    \"hey\",\n    \"good morning\",\n    \"good afternoon\",\n    \"good evening\",\n    \"namaste\",\n}\nGREETING_ALLOWED_TOKENS = {\n    \"hi\",\n    \"hello\",\n    \"hey\",\n    \"good\",\n    \"morning\",\n    \"afternoon\",\n    \"evening\",\n    \"namaste\",\n    \"there\",\n    \"team\",\n    \"metrosense\",\n    \"metro\",\n    \"sense\",\n    \"agent\",\n}\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass IntentClassification:\n    flood: bool\n    heat: bool\n    infra: bool\n    logistics: bool\n\n\ndef _matches_any(text: str, keywords: set[str]) -> bool:\n    return any(keyword in text for keyword in keywords)\n\n\ndef classify_intents(message: str) -> IntentClassification:\n    text = message.lower()\n    return IntentClassification(\n        flood=_matches_any(text, FLOOD_KEYWORDS),\n        heat=_matches_any(text, HEAT_KEYWORDS),\n        infra=_matches_any(text, INFRA_KEYWORDS),\n        logistics=_matches_any(text, LOGISTICS_KEYWORDS),\n    )\n\n\ndef should_trigger_scorecard(message: str) -> bool:\n    return _matches_any(message.lower(), SCORECARD_KEYWORDS)\n\n\ndef determine_response_mode(message: str) -> str:\n    if should_trigger_scorecard(message):\n        return \"scorecard\"\n    return \"text\"\n\n\ndef _normalize_text(message: str) -> str:\n    lowered = message.lower().strip()\n    cleaned = re.sub(r\"[^a-z0-9\\s]\", \" \", lowered)\n    return \" \".join(cleaned.split())\n\n\ndef is_greeting_only(message: str) -> bool:\n    normalized = _normalize_text(message)\n    if not normalized:\n        return False\n\n    if normalized in GREETING_PHRASES:\n        return True\n\n    # If query intent words exist, this is not greeting-only.\n    if _matches_any(normalized, FLOOD_KEYWORDS | HEAT_KEYWORDS | INFRA_KEYWORDS | LOGISTICS_KEYWORDS):\n        return False\n    if _matches_any(normalized, SCORECARD_KEYWORDS):\n        return False\n\n    tokens = set(normalized.split())\n    contains_greeting_token = any(token in GREETING_ALLOWED_TOKENS for token in tokens) and (\n        \"hi\" in tokens\n        or \"hello\" in tokens\n        or \"hey\" in tokens\n        or \"namaste\" in tokens\n        or (\"good\" in tokens and (\"morning\" in tokens or \"afternoon\" in tokens or \"evening\" in tokens))\n    )\n    return contains_greeting_token and tokens.issubset(GREETING_ALLOWED_TOKENS)\n"
  }
}
```

value: string; severity: keyof typeof severityStyles; progress: number }) {\n  const palette = severityStyles[severity];\n  return (\n    <Stack spacing={0.75}>\n      <Stack direction=\"row\" justifyContent=\"space-between\" alignItems=\"center\" gap={2}>\n        <Typography fontFamily='\"IBM Plex Mono\", monospace' fontSize={12} letterSpacing=\"0.08em\">\n          {label}\n        </Typography>\n        <Stack direction=\"row\" spacing={1} alignItems=\"center\">\n          <Typography fontWeight={600}>{value}</Typography>\n          <Box sx={{ backgroundColor: palette.bg, color: palette.text, px: 1, py: 0.3, borderRadius: 10, fontSize: 12, fontWeight: 700 }}>\n            {severity}\n          </Box>\n        </Stack>\n      </Stack>\n      <LinearProgress\n        variant=\"determinate\"\n        value={Math.max(0, Math.min(100, progress))}\n        sx={{\n          height: 8,\n          borderRadius: 999,\n          backgroundColor: \"#EEF2F7\",\n          \"& .MuiLinearProgress-bar\": { backgroundColor: palette.bar },\n        }}\n      />\n    </Stack>\n  );\n}\n\nexport default function RiskCard({ riskCard }: RiskCardProps) {\n  return (\n    <Box sx={{ border: \"1px solid #D1DBE8\", borderRadius: 2, overflow: \"hidden\", backgroundColor: \"#FFFFFF\", boxShadow: \"0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.06)\" }}>\n      <Stack direction=\"row\" justifyContent=\"space-between\" alignItems=\"center\" sx={{ px: 2, py: 1.5, backgroundColor: \"#F7F9FC\" }}>\n        <Typography fontFamily='\"IBM Plex Mono\", monospace' fontWeight={700}>\n          {riskCard.neighborhood}\n        </Typography>\n        <Typography fontWeight={700}>Risk Score: {riskCard.overall_risk_score.toFixed(1)}</Typography>\n      </Stack>\n\n      <Stack spacing={1.5} sx={{ px: 2, py: 2 }}>\n        {riskCard.flood_risk ? (\n          <MetricRow\n            label=\"FLOOD\"\n            value={`${Math.round((riskCard.flood_risk.probability ?? 0) * 100)}%`}\n            severity={(riskCard.flood_risk.severity ?? \"LOW\") as keyof typeof severityStyles}\n            progress={(riskCard.flood_risk.probability ?? 0) * 100}\n          />\n        ) : null}\n        {riskCard.power_outage_risk ? (\n          <MetricRow\n            label=\"POWER OUTAGE\"\n            value={`${Math.round((riskCard.power_outage_risk.probability ?? 0) * 100)}%`}\n            severity={(riskCard.power_outage_risk.severity ?? \"LOW\") as keyof typeof severityStyles}\n            progress={(riskCard.power_outage_risk.probability ?? 0) * 100}\n          />\n        ) : null}\n        {riskCard.traffic_delay_index ? (\n          <MetricRow\n            label=\"TRAFFIC\"\n            value={`${(riskCard.traffic_delay_index.congestion_score ?? 0).toFixed(1)}`}\n            severity={(riskCard.traffic_delay_index.severity ?? \"LOW\") as keyof typeof severityStyles}\n            progress={((riskCard.traffic_delay_index.congestion_score ?? 0) / 10) * 100}\n          />\n        ) : null}\n        {riskCard.health_advisory ? (\n          <MetricRow\n            label=\"AQI\"\n            value={`${riskCard.health_advisory.aqi ?? 0}`}\n            severity={riskCard.health_advisory.aqi && riskCard.health_advisory.aqi >= 150 ? \"HIGH\" : riskCard.health_advisory.aqi && riskCard.health_advisory.aqi >= 100 ? \"MODERATE\" : \"LOW\"}\n            progress={Math.min(100, ((riskCard.health_advisory.aqi ?? 0) / 300) * 100)}\n          />\n        ) : null}\n      </Stack>\n\n      {riskCard.emergency_readiness ? (\n        <>\n          <Divider />\n          <Box sx={{ px: 2, py: 1.5, backgroundColor: \"#F0F6FF\" }}>\n            <Typography fontWeight={600}>{riskCard.emergency_readiness.recommendation}</Typography>\n            {riskCard.emergency_readiness.actions?.length ? (\n              <Box component=\"ul\" sx={{ mb: 0, mt: 1, pl: 2.5 }}>\n                {riskCard.emergency_readiness.actions.map((action) => (\n                  <Typography key={action} component=\"li\" sx={{ mb: 0.5 }}>\n                    {action}\n                  </Typography>\n                ))}\n              </Box>\n            ) : null}\n          </Box>\n        </>\n      ) : null}\n\n      {riskCard.barricade_recommendations?.length ? (\n        <>\n          <Divider />\n          <Box sx={{ px: 2, py: 1.5 }}>\n            <Stack direction=\"row\" alignItems=\"center\" spacing={1.5} sx={{ mb: 1 }}>\n              <Typography fontFamily='\"IBM Plex Mono\", monospace' fontWeight={700} fontSize={12} letterSpacing=\"0.08em\">\n                BARRICADE RECOMMENDATIONS\n              </Typography>\n              {riskCard.rainfall_expected_mm_per_hr != null ? (\n                <Chip\n                  label={`${riskCard.rainfall_expected_mm_per_hr} mm/hr · ${riskCard.rainfall_classification ?? \"Heavy\"}`}\n                  size=\"small\"\n                  sx={{ backgroundColor: \"#FFF0F2\", color: \"#9B1D2A\", fontWeight: 700, fontSize: 11 }}\n                />\n              ) : null}\n            </Stack>\n            <Table size=\"small\" sx={{ tableLayout: \"fixed\" }}>\n              <TableHead>\n                <TableRow sx={{ backgroundColor: \"#F7F9FC\" }}>\n                  <TableCell sx={{ fontWeight: 700, width: \"45%\", borderBottom: \"2px solid #D1DBE8\", fontSize: 12 }}>Underpass / Junction</TableCell>\n                  <TableCell sx={{ fontWeight: 700, borderBottom: \"2px solid #D1DBE8\", fontSize: 12 }}>Trigger Reason</TableCell>\n                </TableRow>\n              </TableHead>\n              <TableBody>\n                {riskCard.barricade_recommendations.map((rec, i) => (\n                  <TableRow\n                    key={rec.underpass_name}\n                    sx={{ backgroundColor: i % 2 === 0 ? \"#FFFFFF\" : \"#F7F9FC\", \"&:last-child td\": { border: 0 } }}\n                  >\n                    <TableCell sx={{ fontWeight: 600, fontSize: 13, verticalAlign: \"top\", py: 1 }}>{rec.underpass_name}</TableCell>\n                    <TableCell sx={{ fontSize: 13, color: \"text.secondary\", verticalAlign: \"top\", py: 1 }}>{rec.reason}</TableCell>\n                  </TableRow>\n                ))}\n              </TableBody>\n            </Table>\n          </Box>\n        </>\n      ) : null}\n    </Box>\n  );\n}\n"
  }
}

{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:20:28.438Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_message",
    "message": "I’ve confirmed the other concrete schema mismatch: the prompt’s `risk_card` example is not the backend/client schema, so it can trigger another validation failure as soon as the agent starts returning cards. I’m fixing this in two places: backend compatibility normalization for legacy cards, and the prompt itself so new responses use the canonical shape.",
    "phase": "commentary"
  }
}
```

        "text": "I’ve confirmed the other concrete schema mismatch: the prompt’s `risk_card` example is not the backend/client schema, so it can trigger another validation failure as soon as the agent starts returning cards.     "phase": "commentary"
  }
}

    "phase": "commentary"

}

## Entry 125
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T18:20:59.066Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:20:59.066Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_Kv4TP2cFKCVhOyzRtm5hC9BF",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/app/services/agent_proxy.py\n@@\n def _normalise_citations(value: Any) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n     \"\"\"Ensure citations_summary is always a list of objects for Pydantic validation.\"\"\"\n     if not isinstance(value, list):\n         return []\n@@\n             normalised.append({\"source\": item})\n     return normalised\n+\n+\n+def _normalise_severity(value: Any) -> str | None:\n+    if not isinstance(value, str) or not value.strip():\n+        return None\n+    return value.strip().upper()\n+\n+\n+def _normalise_risk_metric(value: Any) -> dict[str, Any] | None:\n+    if not isinstance(value, dict):\n+        return None\n+\n+    probability = value.get(\"probability\")\n+    score = value.get(\"score\")\n+    congestion_score = value.get(\"congestion_score\")\n+\n+    normalised: dict[str, Any] = {\n+        \"probability\": probability if isinstance(probability, (int, float)) else None,\n+        \"severity\": _normalise_severity(value.get(\"severity\") or value.get(\"label\")),\n+        \"congestion_score\": (\n+            congestion_score if isinstance(congestion_score, (int, float)) else None\n+        ),\n+    }\n+\n+    if normalised[\"probability\"] is None and isinstance(score, (int, float)):\n+        normalised[\"probability\"] = max(0.0, min(1.0, float(score) / 10.0))\n+\n+    if normalised[\"congestion_score\"] is None and isinstance(score, (int, float)):\n+        normalised[\"congestion_score\"] = float(score)\n+\n+    if all(item is None for item in normalised.values()):\n+        return None\n+    return normalised\n+\n+\n+def _normalise_health_advisory(value: Any) -> dict[str, Any] | None:\n+    if not isinstance(value, dict):\n+        return None\n+\n+    advisory = {\n+        \"aqi\": value.get(\"aqi\") if isinstance(value.get(\"aqi\"), int) else None,\n+        \"aqi_category\": value.get(\"aqi_category\")\n+        if isinstance(value.get(\"aqi_category\"), str)\n+        else None,\n+    }\n+    if advisory[\"aqi\"] is None and advisory[\"aqi_category\"] is None:\n+        return None\n+    return advisory\n+\n+\n+def _normalise_emergency_readiness(value: Any, advisory: Any) -> dict[str, Any] | None:\n+    if not isinstance(value, dict):\n+        if isinstance(advisory, str) and advisory.strip():\n+            return {\"recommendation\": advisory.strip(), \"actions\": None}\n+        return None\n+\n+    recommendation = value.get(\"recommendation\")\n+    if not isinstance(recommendation, str) or not recommendation.strip():\n+        if isinstance(value.get(\"detail\"), str) and value[\"detail\"].strip():\n+            recommendation = value[\"detail\"].strip()\n+        elif isinstance(advisory, str) and advisory.strip():\n+            recommendation = advisory.strip()\n+        elif isinstance(value.get(\"label\"), str) and value[\"label\"].strip():\n+            recommendation = value[\"label\"].strip()\n+        else:\n+            return None\n+\n+    actions = value.get(\"actions\")\n+    if not isinstance(actions, list):\n+        actions = None\n+    else:\n+        actions = [item for item in actions if isinstance(item, str) and item.strip()]\n+\n+    return {\"recommendation\": recommendation, \"actions\": actions or None}\n+\n+\n+def _compute_overall_risk_score(card: dict[str, Any]) -> float:\n+    raw_score = card.get(\"overall_risk_score\")\n+    if isinstance(raw_score, (int, float)):\n+        return float(raw_score)\n+\n+    component_scores: list[float] = []\n+    for key in (\"flood_risk\", \"power_outage_risk\", \"traffic_delay_index\"):\n+        metric = card.get(key)\n+        if isinstance(metric, dict):\n+            score = metric.get(\"congestion_score\")\n+            if key != \"traffic_delay_index\":\n+                probability = metric.get(\"probability\")\n+                if isinstance(probability, (int, float)):\n+                    score = float(probability) * 10.0\n+            if isinstance(score, (int, float)):\n+                component_scores.append(float(score))\n+\n+    readiness = card.get(\"emergency_readiness\")\n+    if isinstance(readiness, dict):\n+        readiness_score = readiness.get(\"score\")\n+        if isinstance(readiness_score, (int, float)):\n+            component_scores.append(max(0.0, 10.0 - float(readiness_score)))\n+\n+    if not component_scores:\n+        return 0.0\n+    return round(sum(component_scores) / len(component_scores), 1)\n+\n+\n+def _normalise_risk_card(value: Any) -> dict[str, Any] | None:\n+    if not isinstance(value, dict):\n+        return None\n+\n+    flood_risk = _normalise_risk_metric(value.get(\"flood_risk\"))\n+    power_outage_risk = _normalise_risk_metric(\n+        value.get(\"power_outage_risk\") or value.get(\"outage_risk\")\n+    )\n+    traffic_delay_index = _normalise_risk_metric(value.get(\"traffic_delay_index\"))\n+    health_advisory = _normalise_health_advisory(value.get(\"health_advisory\"))\n+    emergency_readiness = _normalise_emergency_readiness(\n+        value.get(\"emergency_readiness\"),\n+        value.get(\"advisory\"),\n+    )\n+\n+    rainfall_expected = value.get(\"rainfall_expected_mm_per_hr\")\n+    if not isinstance(rainfall_expected, (int, float)):\n+        rainfall_expected = None\n+\n+    rainfall_classification = value.get(\"rainfall_classification\")\n+    if not isinstance(rainfall_classification, str):\n+        rainfall_classification = None\n+\n+    barricades = value.get(\"barricade_recommendations\")\n+    if isinstance(barricades, list):\n+        barricades = [\n+            item\n+            for item in barricades\n+            if isinstance(item, dict)\n+            and isinstance(item.get(\"underpass_name\"), str)\n+            and isinstance(item.get(\"reason\"), str)\n+        ]\n+    else:\n+        barricades = None\n+\n+    card = {\n+        \"neighborhood\": value.get(\"neighborhood\") or value.get(\"location\") or \"Unknown\",\n+        \"generated_at\": value.get(\"generated_at\") or value.get(\"as_of\") or \"\",\n+        \"overall_risk_score\": 0.0,\n+        \"flood_risk\": flood_risk,\n+        \"power_outage_risk\": power_outage_risk,\n+        \"traffic_delay_index\": traffic_delay_index,\n+        \"health_advisory\": health_advisory,\n+        \"emergency_readiness\": emergency_readiness,\n+        \"rainfall_expected_mm_per_hr\": float(rainfall_expected) if rainfall_expected is not None else None,\n+        \"rainfall_classification\": rainfall_classification,\n+        \"barricade_recommendations\": barricades or None,\n+    }\n+    card[\"overall_risk_score\"] = _compute_overall_risk_score({**value, **card})\n+    return card\n@@\n     if isinstance(response_text, str) and response_text.strip():\n         return {\n             \"session_id\": session_id,\n             \"response_mode\": parsed.get(\"response_mode\", \"text\"),\n             \"response_text\": response_text,\n             \"citations_summary\": _normalise_citations(parsed.get(\"citations_summary\", [])),\n             \"data_freshness_summary\": _normalise_freshness(parsed.get(\"data_freshness_summary\")),\n-            \"risk_card\": parsed.get(\"risk_card\"),\n+            \"risk_card\": _normalise_risk_card(parsed.get(\"risk_card\")),\n             \"artifact\": parsed.get(\"artifact\"),\n             \"follow_up_prompt\": parsed.get(\"follow_up_prompt\"),\n             \"message\": response_text,\n         }\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}

value.strip().upper()\n+\n+\n+def _normalise_risk_metric(value: Any) -> dict[str, Any] | None:\n+    if not isinstance(value, dict):\n+        return None\n+\n+    probability = value.get(\"probability\")\n+    score = value.get(\"score\")\n+    congestion_score = value.get(\"congestion_score\")\n+\n+    normalised: dict[str, Any] = {\n+        \"probability\": probability if isinstance(probability, (int, float)) else None,\n+        \"severity\": _normalise_severity(value.get(\"severity\") or value.get(\"label\")),\n+        \"congestion_score\": (\n+            congestion_score if isinstance(congestion_score, (int, float)) else None\n+        ),\n+    }\n+\n+    if normalised[\"probability\"] is None and isinstance(score, (int, float)):\n+        normalised[\"probability\"] = max(0.0, min(1.0, float(score) / 10.0))\n+\n+    if normalised[\"congestion_score\"] is None and isinstance(score, (int, float)):\n+        normalised[\"congestion_score\"] = float(score)\n+\n+    if all(item is None for item in normalised.values()):\n+        return None\n+    return normalised\n+\n+\n+def _normalise_health_advisory(value: Any) -> dict[str, Any] | None:\n+    if not isinstance(value, dict):\n+        return None\n+\n+    advisory = {\n+        \"aqi\": value.get(\"aqi\") if isinstance(value.get(\"aqi\"), int) else None,\n+        \"aqi_category\": value.get(\"aqi_category\")\n+        if isinstance(value.get(\"aqi_category\"), str)\n+        else None,\n+    }\n+    if advisory[\"aqi\"] is None and advisory[\"aqi_category\"] is None:\n+        return None\n+    return advisory\n+\n+\n+def _normalise_emergency_readiness(value: Any, advisory: Any) -> dict[str, Any] | None:\n+    if not isinstance(value, dict):\n+        if isinstance(advisory, str) and advisory.strip():\n+            return {\"recommendation\": advisory.strip(), \"actions\": None}\n+        return None\n+\n+    recommendation = value.get(\"recommendation\")\n+    if not isinstance(recommendation, str) or not recommendation.strip():\n+        if isinstance(value.get(\"detail\"), str) and value[\"detail\"].strip():\n+            recommendation = value[\"detail\"].strip()\n+        elif isinstance(advisory, str) and advisory.strip():\n+            recommendation = advisory.strip()\n+        elif isinstance(value.get(\"label\"), str) and value[\"label\"].strip():\n+            recommendation = value[\"label\"].strip()\n+        else:\n+            return None\n+\n+    actions = value.get(\"actions\")\n+    if not isinstance(actions, list):\n+        actions = None\n+    else:\n+        actions = [item for item in actions if isinstance(item, str) and item.strip()]\n+\n+    return {\"recommendation\": recommendation, \"actions\": actions or None}\n+\n+\n+def _compute_overall_risk_score(card: dict[str, Any]) -> float:\n+    raw_score = card.get(\"overall_risk_score\")\n+    if isinstance(raw_score, (int, float)):\n+        return float(raw_score)\n+\n+    component_scores: list[float] = []\n+    for key in (\"flood_risk\", \"power_outage_risk\", \"traffic_delay_index\"):\n+        metric = card.get(key)\n+        if isinstance(metric, dict):\n+            score = metric.get(\"congestion_score\")\n+            if key != \"traffic_delay_index\":\n+                probability = metric.get(\"probability\")\n+                if isinstance(probability, (int, float)):\n+                    score = float(probability) * 10.0\n+            if isinstance(score, (int, float)):\n+                component_scores.append(float(score))\n+\n+    readiness = card.get(\"emergency_readiness\")\n+    if isinstance(readiness, dict):\n+        readiness_score = readiness.get(\"score\")\n+        if isinstance(readiness_score, (int, float)):\n+            component_scores.append(max(0.0, 10.0 - float(readiness_score)))\n+\n+    if not component_scores:\n+        return 0.0\n+    return round(sum(component_scores) / len(component_scores), 1)\n+\n+\n+def _normalise_risk_card(value: Any) -> dict[str, Any] | None:\n+    if not isinstance(value, dict):\n+        return None\n+\n+    flood_risk = _normalise_risk_metric(value.get(\"flood_risk\"))\n+    power_outage_risk = _normalise_risk_metric(\n+        value.get(\"power_outage_risk\") or value.get(\"outage_risk\")\n+    )\n+    traffic_delay_index = _normalise_risk_metric(value.get(\"traffic_delay_index\"))\n+    health_advisory = _normalise_health_advisory(value.get(\"health_advisory\"))\n+    emergency_readiness = _normalise_emergency_readiness(\n+        value.get(\"emergency_readiness\"),\n+        value.get(\"advisory\"),\n+    )\n+\n+    rainfall_expected = value.get(\"rainfall_expected_mm_per_hr\")\n+    if not isinstance(rainfall_expected, (int, float)):\n+        rainfall_expected = None\n+\n+    rainfall_classification = value.get(\"rainfall_classification\")\n+    if not isinstance(rainfall_classification, str):\n+        rainfall_classification = None\n+\n+    barricades = value.get(\"barricade_recommendations\")\n+    if isinstance(barricades, list):\n+        barricades = [\n+            item\n+            for item in barricades\n+            if isinstance(item, dict)\n+            and isinstance(item.get(\"underpass_name\"), str)\n+            and isinstance(item.get(\"reason\"), str)\n+        ]\n+    else:\n+        barricades = None\n+\n+    card = {\n+        \"neighborhood\": value.get(\"neighborhood\") or value.get(\"location\") or \"Unknown\",\n+        \"generated_at\": value.get(\"generated_at\") or value.get(\"as_of\") or \"\",\n+        \"overall_risk_score\": 0.0,\n+        \"flood_risk\": flood_risk,\n+        \"power_outage_risk\": power_outage_risk,\n+        \"traffic_delay_index\": traffic_delay_index,\n+        \"health_advisory\": health_advisory,\n+        \"emergency_readiness\": emergency_readiness,\n+        \"rainfall_expected_mm_per_hr\": float(rainfall_expected) if rainfall_expected is not None else None,\n+        \"rainfall_classification\": rainfall_classification,\n+        \"barricade_recommendations\": barricades or None,\n+    }\n+    card[\"overall_risk_score\"] = _compute_overall_risk_score({**value, **card})\n+    return card\n@@\n     if isinstance(response_text, str) and response_text.strip():\n         return {\n             \"session_id\": session_id,\n             \"response_mode\": parsed.get(\"response_mode\", \"text\"),\n             \"response_text\": response_text,\n             \"citations_summary\": _normalise_citations(parsed.get(\"citations_summary\", [])),\n             \"data_freshness_summary\": _normalise_freshness(parsed.get(\"data_freshness_summary\")),\n-            \"risk_card\": parsed.get(\"risk_card\"),\n+            \"risk_card\": _normalise_risk_card(parsed.get(\"risk_card\")),\n             \"artifact\": parsed.get(\"artifact\"),\n             \"follow_up_prompt\": parsed.get(\"follow_up_prompt\"),\n             \"message\": response_text,\n         }\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 168
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T18:22:05.098Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:22:05.098Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_message",
    "message": "I found and fixed the other concrete schema mismatch that could fail the same way.\n\nIn addition to `citations_summary`, the agent prompt was also emitting a legacy `risk_card` shape that does not match the backend/client contract. The backend expects `neighborhood`, `generated_at`, `overall_risk_score`, `power_outage_risk`, etc., while the prompt still described `location`, `as_of`, `outage_risk`, `score`, `label`, and `advisory`. That would have triggered another `ValidationError` as soon as the agent returned a card.\n\nI fixed this in two places:\n- [agent_proxy.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/app/services/agent_proxy.py): added normalization for legacy agent output.\n  - string citations -> `{\"source\": ...}`\n  - legacy `risk_card` -> canonical backend shape\n  - severity strings normalized to uppercase for the frontend\n  - fallback `overall_risk_score` derived when only legacy component scores are present\n- [chat.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/prompts/chat.py): updated the prompt so the agent now emits the canonical schema directly.\n\nI also added regression coverage in [test_agent_proxy.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/tests/unit/test_agent_proxy.py) for both:\n- string `citations_summary`\n- legacy `risk_card` payloads\n\nVerification:\n```bash\ncd server\nuv run pytest tests/unit/test_agent_proxy.py tests/unit/test_chat_api.py -q\nuv run ruff check app/services/agent_proxy.py tests/unit/test_agent_proxy.py\n```\nResult: `13 passed`, Ruff clean.\n\nIf you want, I can do one more pass specifically on `artifact` and any agent Level-1 payloads to harden them the same way.",
    "phase": "final_answer"
  }
}
```

{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:29:41.891Z",
  "type": "turn_context",
  "payload": {
    "turn_id": "019cde29-697f-7ed3-b0f1-2fcad504e29e",
    "cwd": "/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense",
    "current_date": "2026-03-11",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "writable_roots": [
        "/home/dell/.codex/memories"
      ],
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.4",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "default",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.4",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\n\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\n\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\n\n## request_user_input availability\n\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\n\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\n"
      }
    },
    "realtime_active": false,
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "none",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Project Identity\n- This repository is the working MetroSense codebase: `client/` for React, `server/` for FastAPI, PostgreSQL via Docker Compose, and CI/quality gates.\n- The product is `MetroSense`, a chat-based climate and infrastructure intelligence system for Bengaluru.\n- The target architecture is a three-service system:\n  - `client/`: frontend chat application\n  - `server/`: public backend API and orchestration layer\n  - `agents/`: internal agent server and tool-execution layer (✅ **exists and wired**, current status: skeleton with google_search tool only)\n- Treat the current implementation as the baseline and evolve it toward this three-service design instead of creating parallel app structures.\n- **Status**: ~70% complete. Auth flow, backend-to-agent routing, database schema, internal data routes, custom domain agent tools, structured response contract, and full subagent hierarchy are functional. Data seeding (script ready, not yet run), streaming, and E2E tests remain.\n\n## Canonical Architecture\n- The intended request flow is:\n  - `frontend -> backend -> agent`\n- The frontend should never call the agent service directly.\n- `server/` is the only public API boundary for browser clients.\n- `agents/` is an internal service boundary responsible for agent runtime behavior, tool usage, and structured chat output generation.\n- If existing docs still use `agent/` singular, treat that as referring to the planned `agents/` subsystem until naming is aligned across the repo.\n\n## Why This Boundary Exists\n- Keep secrets, database access, internal tools, and agent infrastructure off the public internet.\n- Let the backend own request validation, session coordination, auth, rate limiting, logging, and response shaping.\n- Preserve a stable frontend contract even if the agent framework, prompting strategy, or toolchain changes.\n- Keep observability and failure handling centralized in the backend rather than split between browser and agent server.\n\n## Tech Stack\n- Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic, PostgreSQL 16, Loguru, `uv`\n- Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite 5, MUI v6, React Query, Zustand, Axios, `pnpm`\n- Tooling: Ruff, MyPy strict, import-linter, ESLint, Docker, Docker Compose, GitHub Actions\n\n## Repository Shape\n- `client/`\n  - Chat UI, streaming presentation, structured response rendering, browser-facing state management.\n- `server/`\n  - Public HTTP API, request validation, orchestration, session handling, backend-to-agent coordination.\n- `agents/`\n  - Internal agent service for chat generation, tool calls, retrieval, and domain intelligence.\n- `server/app/api/`\n  - FastAPI routes and request/response boundaries.\n- `server/app/services/`\n  - Backend business logic and internal orchestration code.\n- `server/app/db/`\n  - Database models, metadata, and session management.\n- `server/tests/unit/`\n  - Fast isolated tests for backend logic and simple route behavior.\n- `server/tests/integration/`\n  - App-level and database-backed tests.\n- `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`\n  - Source of truth for the planned MetroSense chat UX and structured response behavior.\n\n## Architectural Rules\n- Preserve backend layering:\n  - `app.api` may depend on `app.services` and `app.core`\n  - `app.services` may depend on `app.db` and `app.core`\n  - `app.db` must not depend on `app.services` or `app.api`\n- Keep the frontend focused on rendering and interaction, not business orchestration.\n- Route all browser chat traffic through `server/`, even after `agents/` exists.\n- Treat backend-to-agent communication as an internal interface that may evolve without breaking the UI.\n- Prefer extending existing `server/` and `client/` entrypoints over introducing duplicate service layers or alternative public APIs.\n\n## Authentication\n- Public endpoints:\n  - `POST /api/auth/signup`\n  - `POST /api/auth/login`\n- Protected endpoints:\n  - All other `/api/*` routes require a valid JWT cookie.\n- `/health` remains public for uptime checks.\n\n## Product Direction\n- MetroSense is intended to be a chat-first application for weather risk, flooding, AQI, outage, traffic, and infrastructure-readiness questions.\n- Expected response modes:\n  - streamed assistant text for most responses\n  - agent-emitted `RiskCard` payloads for structured neighborhood risk outputs\n  - agent-emitted `Artifact` payloads for charts, maps, tables, or other visual responses\n- Structured outputs should be decided by the agent layer and delivered through the backend to the frontend.\n- The frontend must render structured responses based on explicit backend/agent event types, not keyword inference.\n\n## Testing And Validation\n- Backend checks from `server/`:\n  - `uv sync --all-extras`\n  - `uv run ruff format --check .`\n  - `uv run ruff check .`\n  - `uv run mypy .`\n  - `uv run lint-imports`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/unit -q`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/integration -q`\n- Agents checks from `agents/`:\n  - `uv sync`\n  - `uv run python scripts/smoke_test.py`\n- Frontend checks from `client/`:\n  - `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`\n  - `pnpm run lint`\n  - `pnpm run build`\n  - `pnpm exec playwright install`\n  - `pnpm run test:e2e`\n- Local startup today:\n  - The agents service runs behind a lightweight proxy that enforces the `X-Internal-Token` shared secret. The backend calls the proxy on port 8020, and the proxy forwards to the ADK server on port 8021. `/health` stays public for simple availability checks.\n  - `docker-compose up -d db`\n  - `cd server && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=your-internal-token-here && uv run alembic upgrade head && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=your-internal-token-here && uv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8021 .`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=your-internal-token-here && uv run uvicorn app.proxy:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020`\n  - `cd agents && uv run adk web --port 8001` (optional UI)\n  - `cd client && pnpm install && pnpm run dev`\n- When expanding `agents/`, keep startup and validation commands updated in this file.\n\n## Current Coverage And Next-Step Expectations\n\n### Implemented (✅)\n- **Backend:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout/me with JWT + httponly cookies), health checks, database schema (10+ models for weather/AQI/flooding/outages/traffic, sessions, conversation history), `/api/chat` endpoint with full `RiskCardPayload` and `ArtifactPayload` response schema\n- **Backend Internal Routes:** All `/internal/*` data routes (weather, AQI, lakes, floods, outages, traffic, ward profile, locations) — token-gated, consumed only by agent tools\n- **Backend Services:** `data_service.py` (all domain DB queries + neighbourhood→zone mapping), `conversation_service.py` (session upsert + turn append to `ConversationHistory`), `agent_proxy.py` (Level-2 and Level-1 ADK response parsing)\n- **Backend-to-Agent:** Proxy service with `X-Internal-Token` validation, session routing to ADK\n- **Agent Subagent Hierarchy:** Five-agent system (`root_agent → chat_agent → flood_vulnerability_agent / heat_health_agent / infrastructure_agent / logistics_agent`) using `gemini-2.5-flash`\n- **Agent Tools:** `flood_tools` (lake hydrology, flood incidents), `heat_tools` (AQI current/historical, ward profile), `infra_tools` (power outage events), `logistics_tools` (traffic current/corridor), `shared/weather_tools` (weather current/historical), `shared/location_tools` (resolve/list locations), `shared/document_tools` (list indexes, fetch section)\n- **Orchestration:** `routing.py` (keyword-based intent classification: flood/heat/infra/logistics/greeting), `response_contract.py` (`build_level2_response()` structured output builder)\n- **Prompts:** Domain-specific system prompts for all five agents; `CHAT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION` mandates JSON-only structured output with data freshness caveats and off-domain refusal\n- **Data Loader Script:** `server/scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py` — reads 6 CSVs and inserts reference + domain tables; run with `uv run python scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py --replace` (not yet executed)\n- **Frontend:** Chat UI (MessageBubble, MessageList, InputBar, ThinkingIndicator, SuggestedPrompts), auth context (login/signup/logout), RiskCard and ArtifactRenderer components, health polling\n- **Tests:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout/me), password hashing, health checks, basic chat endpoint\n\n### Planned (📋)\n- **Data Seeding:** `load_metrosense_dataset.py` exists and is ready to run; CSVs not yet loaded into PostgreSQL — run once before agent tools can return real data\n- **Document Ingestion:** Reference documents (`Documents_Metrosense/`) are read directly from the filesystem by `document_tools`; no DB ingestion needed\n- **Streaming:** Responses are buffered POST/response; SSE or WebSocket streaming not yet implemented\n- **Message Context Loading:** `ConversationHistory` model and `conversation_service` exist; conversation context not yet injected into agent session on new requests\n- **Structured Risk Card Generation:** End-to-end schema is wired (`RiskCardPayload` in routes, `build_level2_response()` in agents); requires data seeding + agent logic to populate fields\n- **E2E Tests:** Playwright configured; no test suites written yet\n- **Chat Response Tests:** Coverage for agent output parsing, risk card generation, artifact rendering\n\n### Current Test Coverage\n- Backend: Health, auth (signup/login/logout/me), password hashing, basic chat routing\n- Frontend: Component renders (no E2E tests written yet)\n- Agents: smoke_test.py validates proxy routing only (not domain logic)\n\n### Next Steps (Recommended Order)\n1. **Run data loader** — `cd server && uv run python scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py --replace` to seed CSVs into PostgreSQL so agent tools return real data\n2. **Validate agent tool responses** — Smoke-test each domain tool end-to-end (internal routes → data_service → PostgreSQL) after seeding\n3. **Wire conversation context** — Pass recent `ConversationHistory` turns to the agent session so the agent has chat memory\n4. **Validate structured output** — Confirm `build_level2_response()` + `RiskCardPayload` round-trip produces renderable frontend cards\n5. **Implement streaming** — Add SSE or WebSocket to `/api/chat` for incremental text delivery\n6. **Write E2E tests** — Playwright suites covering login → chat → risk card render\n\n## Agent Guidance\n- Before major implementation work, verify repo truth from `README.md`, `server/pyproject.toml`, `client/package.json`, and `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`.\n- Prefer `rg` for search.\n- Do not document or reference unimplemented services as if they already exist.\n- **Important:** The CSV data files (`DataSet_MetroSense/`) exist in the repo and the loader script (`server/scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py`) is implemented but **not yet run**. Seed the database before expecting agent domain tools to return real data.\n- Documents (`Documents_Metrosense/`) are served directly from the filesystem by `document_tools` — no DB load needed, but `DOCUMENTS_PATH` env must point to the correct directory.\n- The `metrosense_agent/` is the production agent; `metrosearch_agent/` is a legacy skeleton that can be removed. All five subagents and their domain tools are implemented and wired into the ADK hierarchy.\n- The `response_contract.py` `build_level2_response()` builder and all Pydantic schemas (`RiskCardPayload`, `ArtifactPayload`) are in place end-to-end — the remaining gap is data availability and agent logic populating those fields.\n- If current behavior and MetroSense direction diverge, align new work to the MetroSense direction while keeping transitions explicit and incremental.\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- google-adk-python: Expert guidance on the Google Agent Development Kit (ADK) for Python. Use this skill when the user asks about building agents, using tools, streaming, callbacks, tutorials, deployment, or advanced architecture with the Google ADK in Python. (file: /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/.agents/skills/google-adk-python/SKILL.md)\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}

Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- google-adk-python: Expert guidance on the Google Agent Development Kit (ADK) for Python. Use this skill when the user asks about building agents, using tools, streaming, callbacks, tutorials, deployment, or advanced architecture with the Google ADK in Python. (file: /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/.agents/skills/google-adk-python/SKILL.md)\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}

## Entry 177

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:29:52.469Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_message",
    "message": "The main issue is that the response is not following the user’s request shape.\n\nYou asked for a table, but the system returned:\n- a prose `summary`\n- a raw Python-style `details` dict\n- an extra `health_advisory`\n\nSo the problems are:\n\n| Issue | Why it is a problem |\n|---|---|\n| No table returned | The user explicitly asked for a comparison table. |\n| Internal/raw structure leaked | `details: {...}` looks like backend/tool output, not user-facing formatting. |\n| Wrong response contract | This looks closer to a Level-1/diagnostic payload than the canonical MetroSense chat response. |\n| Extra unsolicited section | `health_advisory` may be useful, but it distracts from the direct request. |\n| Poor frontend renderability | A raw dict is harder for the UI to present cleanly than a structured artifact/table payload. |\n\nWhat it should have done instead:\n- return `response_text` with a short intro\n- include an `artifact` representing a table, or at minimum format the comparison as markdown/plain table\n- keep the weather comparison data user-facing, not as dumped dicts\n\nA correct user-facing result would look more like:\n\n| Metric | September 2025 | October 2025 |\n|---|---:|---:|\n| Avg Temp (C) | 23.3 | 24.3 |\n| Max Temp (C) | 31.5 | 32.2 |\n| Min Temp (C) | 15.1 | 14.0 |\n| Avg Humidity (%) | 89.0 | 78.2 |\n| Total Rainfall (mm) | 697.3 | 208.4 |\n| Readings Count | 2880 | 2976 |\n\nSo yes, the data itself looks plausible, but the response formatting/orchestration is wrong. The agent is answering with semi-structured internal output instead of a properly rendered final response.",
    "phase": "final_answer"
  }
}
```

## Entry 180
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T18:29:52.485Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:29:52.485Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "task_complete",
    "turn_id": "019cde29-697f-7ed3-b0f1-2fcad504e29e",
    "last_agent_message": "The main issue is that the response is not following the user’s request shape.\n\nYou asked for a table, but the system returned:\n- a prose `summary`\n- a raw Python-style `details` dict\n- an extra `health_advisory`\n\nSo the problems are:\n\n| Issue | Why it is a problem |\n|---|---|\n| No table returned | The user explicitly asked for a comparison table. |\n| Internal/raw structure leaked | `details: {...}` looks like backend/tool output, not user-facing formatting. |\n| Wrong response contract | This looks closer to a Level-1/diagnostic payload than the canonical MetroSense chat response. |\n| Extra unsolicited section | `health_advisory` may be useful, but it distracts from the direct request. |\n| Poor frontend renderability | A raw dict is harder for the UI to present cleanly than a structured artifact/table payload. |\n\nWhat it should have done instead:\n- return `response_text` with a short intro\n- include an `artifact` representing a table, or at minimum format the comparison as markdown/plain table\n- keep the weather comparison data user-facing, not as dumped dicts\n\nA correct user-facing result would look more like:\n\n| Metric | September 2025 | October 2025 |\n|---|---:|---:|\n| Avg Temp (C) | 23.3 | 24.3 |\n| Max Temp (C) | 31.5 | 32.2 |\n| Min Temp (C) | 15.1 | 14.0 |\n| Avg Humidity (%) | 89.0 | 78.2 |\n| Total Rainfall (mm) | 697.3 | 208.4 |\n| Readings Count | 2880 | 2976 |\n\nSo yes, the data itself looks plausible, but the response formatting/orchestration is wrong. The agent is answering with semi-structured internal output instead of a properly rendered final response."
  }
}
```

## Entry 183
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T18:30:19.123Z
**Role/Type:** turn_context
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:30:19.123Z",
  "type": "turn_context",
  "payload": {
    "turn_id": "019cde29-faef-7a42-9d1e-f62a11ab100f",
    "cwd": "/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense",
    "current_date": "2026-03-12",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "writable_roots": [
        "/home/dell/.codex/memories"
      ],
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.4",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "default",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.4",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\n\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\n\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\n\n## request_user_input availability\n\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\n\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\n"
      }
    },
    "realtime_active": false,
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "none",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Project Identity\n- This repository is the working MetroSense codebase: `client/` for React, `server/` for FastAPI, PostgreSQL via Docker Compose, and CI/quality gates.\n- The product is `MetroSense`, a chat-based climate and infrastructure intelligence system for Bengaluru.\n- The target architecture is a three-service system:\n  - `client/`: frontend chat application\n  - `server/`: public backend API and orchestration layer\n  - `agents/`: internal agent server and tool-execution layer (✅ **exists and wired**, current status: skeleton with google_search tool only)\n- Treat the current implementation as the baseline and evolve it toward this three-service design instead of creating parallel app structures.\n- **Status**: ~70% complete. Auth flow, backend-to-agent routing, database schema, internal data routes, custom domain agent tools, structured response contract, and full subagent hierarchy are functional. Data seeding (script ready, not yet run), streaming, and E2E tests remain.\n\n## Canonical Architecture\n- The intended request flow is:\n  - `frontend -> backend -> agent`\n- The frontend should never call the agent service directly.\n- `server/` is the only public API boundary for browser clients.\n- `agents/` is an internal service boundary responsible for agent runtime behavior, tool usage, and structured chat output generation.\n- If existing docs still use `agent/` singular, treat that as referring to the planned `agents/` subsystem until naming is aligned across the repo.\n\n## Why This Boundary Exists\n- Keep secrets, database access, internal tools, and agent infrastructure off the public internet.\n- Let the backend own request validation, session coordination, auth, rate limiting, logging, and response shaping.\n- Preserve a stable frontend contract even if the agent framework, prompting strategy, or toolchain changes.\n- Keep observability and failure handling centralized in the backend rather than split between browser and agent server.\n\n## Tech Stack\n- Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic, PostgreSQL 16, Loguru, `uv`\n- Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite 5, MUI v6, React Query, Zustand, Axios, `pnpm`\n- Tooling: Ruff, MyPy strict, import-linter, ESLint, Docker, Docker Compose, GitHub Actions\n\n## Repository Shape\n- `client/`\n  - Chat UI, streaming presentation, structured response rendering, browser-facing state management.\n- `server/`\n  - Public HTTP API, request validation, orchestration, session handling, backend-to-agent coordination.\n- `agents/`\n  - Internal agent service for chat generation, tool calls, retrieval, and domain intelligence.\n- `server/app/api/`\n  - FastAPI routes and request/response boundaries.\n- `server/app/services/`\n  - Backend business logic and internal orchestration code.\n- `server/app/db/`\n  - Database models, metadata, and session management.\n- `server/tests/unit/`\n  - Fast isolated tests for backend logic and simple route behavior.\n- `server/tests/integration/`\n  - App-level and database-backed tests.\n- `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`\n  - Source of truth for the planned MetroSense chat UX and structured response behavior.\n\n## Architectural Rules\n- Preserve backend layering:\n  - `app.api` may depend on `app.services` and `app.core`\n  - `app.services` may depend on `app.db` and `app.core`\n  - `app.db` must not depend on `app.services` or `app.api`\n- Keep the frontend focused on rendering and interaction, not business orchestration.\n- Route all browser chat traffic through `server/`, even after `agents/` exists.\n- Treat backend-to-agent communication as an internal interface that may evolve without breaking the UI.\n- Prefer extending existing `server/` and `client/` entrypoints over introducing duplicate service layers or alternative public APIs.\n\n## Authentication\n- Public endpoints:\n  - `POST /api/auth/signup`\n  - `POST /api/auth/login`\n- Protected endpoints:\n  - All other `/api/*` routes require a valid JWT cookie.\n- `/health` remains public for uptime checks.\n\n## Product Direction\n- MetroSense is intended to be a chat-first application for weather risk, flooding, AQI, outage, traffic, and infrastructure-readiness questions.\n- Expected response modes:\n  - streamed assistant text for most responses\n  - agent-emitted `RiskCard` payloads for structured neighborhood risk outputs\n  - agent-emitted `Artifact` payloads for charts, maps, tables, or other visual responses\n- Structured outputs should be decided by the agent layer and delivered through the backend to the frontend.\n- The frontend must render structured responses based on explicit backend/agent event types, not keyword inference.\n\n## Testing And Validation\n- Backend checks from `server/`:\n  - `uv sync --all-extras`\n  - `uv run ruff format --check .`\n  - `uv run ruff check .`\n  - `uv run mypy .`\n  - `uv run lint-imports`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/unit -q`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/integration -q`\n- Agents checks from `agents/`:\n  - `uv sync`\n  - `uv run python scripts/smoke_test.py`\n- Frontend checks from `client/`:\n  - `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`\n  - `pnpm run lint`\n  - `pnpm run build`\n  - `pnpm exec playwright install`\n  - `pnpm run test:e2e`\n- Local startup today:\n  - The agents service runs behind a lightweight proxy that enforces the `X-Internal-Token` shared secret. The backend calls the proxy on port 8020, and the proxy forwards to the ADK server on port 8021. `/health` stays public for simple availability checks.\n  - `docker-compose up -d db`\n  - `cd server && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=your-internal-token-here && uv run alembic upgrade head && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=your-internal-token-here && uv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8021 .`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=your-internal-token-here && uv run uvicorn app.proxy:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020`\n  - `cd agents && uv run adk web --port 8001` (optional UI)\n  - `cd client && pnpm install && pnpm run dev`\n- When expanding `agents/`, keep startup and validation commands updated in this file.\n\n## Current Coverage And Next-Step Expectations\n\n### Implemented (✅)\n- **Backend:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout/me with JWT + httponly cookies), health checks, database schema (10+ models for weather/AQI/flooding/outages/traffic, sessions, conversation history), `/api/chat` endpoint with full `RiskCardPayload` and `ArtifactPayload` response schema\n- **Backend Internal Routes:** All `/internal/*` data routes (weather, AQI, lakes, floods, outages, traffic, ward profile, locations) — token-gated, consumed only by agent tools\n- **Backend Services:** `data_service.py` (all domain DB queries + neighbourhood→zone mapping), `conversation_service.py` (session upsert + turn append to `ConversationHistory`), `agent_proxy.py` (Level-2 and Level-1 ADK response parsing)\n- **Backend-to-Agent:** Proxy service with `X-Internal-Token` validation, session routing to ADK\n- **Agent Subagent Hierarchy:** Five-agent system (`root_agent → chat_agent → flood_vulnerability_agent / heat_health_agent / infrastructure_agent / logistics_agent`) using `gemini-2.5-flash`\n- **Agent Tools:** `flood_tools` (lake hydrology, flood incidents), `heat_tools` (AQI current/historical, ward profile), `infra_tools` (power outage events), `logistics_tools` (traffic current/corridor), `shared/weather_tools` (weather current/historical), `shared/location_tools` (resolve/list locations), `shared/document_tools` (list indexes, fetch section)\n- **Orchestration:** `routing.py` (keyword-based intent classification: flood/heat/infra/logistics/greeting), `response_contract.py` (`build_level2_response()` structured output builder)\n- **Prompts:** Domain-specific system prompts for all five agents; `CHAT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION` mandates JSON-only structured output with data freshness caveats and off-domain refusal\n- **Data Loader Script:** `server/scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py` — reads 6 CSVs and inserts reference + domain tables; run with `uv run python scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py --replace` (not yet executed)\n- **Frontend:** Chat UI (MessageBubble, MessageList, InputBar, ThinkingIndicator, SuggestedPrompts), auth context (login/signup/logout), RiskCard and ArtifactRenderer components, health polling\n- **Tests:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout/me), password hashing, health checks, basic chat endpoint\n\n### Planned (📋)\n- **Data Seeding:** `load_metrosense_dataset.py` exists and is ready to run; CSVs not yet loaded into PostgreSQL — run once before agent tools can return real data\n- **Document Ingestion:** Reference documents (`Documents_Metrosense/`) are read directly from the filesystem by `document_tools`; no DB ingestion needed\n- **Streaming:** Responses are buffered POST/response; SSE or WebSocket streaming not yet implemented\n- **Message Context Loading:** `ConversationHistory` model and `conversation_service` exist; conversation context not yet injected into agent session on new requests\n- **Structured Risk Card Generation:** End-to-end schema is wired (`RiskCardPayload` in routes, `build_level2_response()` in agents); requires data seeding + agent logic to populate fields\n- **E2E Tests:** Playwright configured; no test suites written yet\n- **Chat Response Tests:** Coverage for agent output parsing, risk card generation, artifact rendering\n\n### Current Test Coverage\n- Backend: Health, auth (signup/login/logout/me), password hashing, basic chat routing\n- Frontend: Component renders (no E2E tests written yet)\n- Agents: smoke_test.py validates proxy routing only (not domain logic)\n\n### Next Steps (Recommended Order)\n1. **Run data loader** — `cd server && uv run python scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py --replace` to seed CSVs into PostgreSQL so agent tools return real data\n2. **Validate agent tool responses** — Smoke-test each domain tool end-to-end (internal routes → data_service → PostgreSQL) after seeding\n3. **Wire conversation context** — Pass recent `ConversationHistory` turns to the agent session so the agent has chat memory\n4. **Validate structured output** — Confirm `build_level2_response()` + `RiskCardPayload` round-trip produces renderable frontend cards\n5. **Implement streaming** — Add SSE or WebSocket to `/api/chat` for incremental text delivery\n6. **Write E2E tests** — Playwright suites covering login → chat → risk card render\n\n## Agent Guidance\n- Before major implementation work, verify repo truth from `README.md`, `server/pyproject.toml`, `client/package.json`, and `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`.\n- Prefer `rg` for search.\n- Do not document or reference unimplemented services as if they already exist.\n- **Important:** The CSV data files (`DataSet_MetroSense/`) exist in the repo and the loader script (`server/scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py`) is implemented but **not yet run**. Seed the database before expecting agent domain tools to return real data.\n- Documents (`Documents_Metrosense/`) are served directly from the filesystem by `document_tools` — no DB load needed, but `DOCUMENTS_PATH` env must point to the correct directory.\n- The `metrosense_agent/` is the production agent; `metrosearch_agent/` is a legacy skeleton that can be removed. All five subagents and their domain tools are implemented and wired into the ADK hierarchy.\n- The `response_contract.py` `build_level2_response()` builder and all Pydantic schemas (`RiskCardPayload`, `ArtifactPayload`) are in place end-to-end — the remaining gap is data availability and agent logic populating those fields.\n- If current behavior and MetroSense direction diverge, align new work to the MetroSense direction while keeping transitions explicit and incremental.\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- google-adk-python: Expert guidance on the Google Agent Development Kit (ADK) for Python. Use this skill when the user asks about building agents, using tools, streaming, callbacks, tutorials, deployment, or advanced architecture with the Google ADK in Python. (file: /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/.agents/skills/google-adk-python/SKILL.md)\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}
```
