    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Project Identity\n- This repository is the working MetroSense codebase: `client/` for React, `server/` for FastAPI, PostgreSQL via Docker Compose, and CI/quality gates.\n- The product is `MetroSense`, a chat-based climate and infrastructure intelligence system for Bengaluru.\n- The target architecture is a three-service system:\n  - `client/`: frontend chat application\n  - `server/`: public backend API and orchestration layer\n  - `agents/`: internal agent server and tool-execution layer (✅ **exists and wired**, current status: skeleton with google_search tool only)\n- Treat the current implementation as the baseline and evolve it toward this three-service design instead of creating parallel app structures.\n- **Status**: ~70% complete. Auth flow, backend-to-agent routing, database schema, internal data routes, custom domain agent tools, structured response contract, and full subagent hierarchy are functional. Data seeding (script ready, not yet run), streaming, and E2E tests remain.\n\n## Canonical Architecture\n- The intended request flow is:\n  - `frontend -> backend -> agent`\n- The frontend should never call the agent service directly.\n- `server/` is the only public API boundary for browser clients.\n- `agents/` is an internal service boundary responsible for agent runtime behavior, tool usage, and structured chat output generation.\n- If existing docs still use `agent/` singular, treat that as referring to the planned `agents/` subsystem until naming is aligned across the repo.\n\n## Why This Boundary Exists\n- Keep secrets, database access, internal tools, and agent infrastructure off the public internet.\n- Let the backend own request validation, session coordination, auth, rate limiting, logging, and response shaping.\n- Preserve a stable frontend contract even if the agent framework, prompting strategy, or toolchain changes.\n- Keep observability and failure handling centralized in the backend rather than split between browser and agent server.\n\n## Tech Stack\n- Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic, PostgreSQL 16, Loguru, `uv`\n- Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite 5, MUI v6, React Query, Zustand, Axios, `pnpm`\n- Tooling: Ruff, MyPy strict, import-linter, ESLint, Docker, Docker Compose, GitHub Actions\n\n## Repository Shape\n- `client/`\n  - Chat UI, streaming presentation, structured response rendering, browser-facing state management.\n- `server/`\n  - Public HTTP API, request validation, orchestration, session handling, backend-to-agent coordination.\n- `agents/`\n  - Internal agent service for chat generation, tool calls, retrieval, and domain intelligence.\n- `server/app/api/`\n  - FastAPI routes and request/response boundaries.\n- `server/app/services/`\n  - Backend business logic and internal orchestration code.\n- `server/app/db/`\n  - Database models, metadata, and session management.\n- `server/tests/unit/`\n  - Fast isolated tests for backend logic and simple route behavior.\n- `server/tests/integration/`\n  - App-level and database-backed tests.\n- `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`\n  - Source of truth for the planned MetroSense chat UX and structured response behavior.\n\n## Architectural Rules\n- Preserve backend layering:\n  - `app.api` may depend on `app.services` and `app.core`\n  - `app.services` may depend on `app.db` and `app.core`\n  - `app.db` must not depend on `app.services` or `app.api`\n- Keep the frontend focused on rendering and interaction, not business orchestration.\n- Route all browser chat traffic through `server/`, even after `agents/` exists.\n- Treat backend-to-agent communication as an internal interface that may evolve without breaking the UI.\n- Prefer extending existing `server/` and `client/` entrypoints over introducing duplicate service layers or alternative public APIs.\n\n## Authentication\n- Public endpoints:\n  - `POST /api/auth/signup`\n  - `POST /api/auth/login`\n- Protected endpoints:\n  - All other `/api/*` routes require a valid JWT cookie.\n- `/health` remains public for uptime checks.\n\n## Product Direction\n- MetroSense is intended to be a chat-first application for weather risk, flooding, AQI, outage, traffic, and infrastructure-readiness questions.\n- Expected response modes:\n  - streamed assistant text for most responses\n  - agent-emitted `RiskCard` payloads for structured neighborhood risk outputs\n  - agent-emitted `Artifact` payloads for charts, maps, tables, or other visual responses\n- Structured outputs should be decided by the agent layer and delivered through the backend to the frontend.\n- The frontend must render structured responses based on explicit backend/agent event types, not keyword inference.\n\n## Testing And Validation\n- Backend checks from `server/`:\n  - `uv sync --all-extras`\n  - `uv run ruff format --check .`\n  - `uv run ruff check .`\n  - `uv run mypy .`\n  - `uv run lint-imports`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/unit -q`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/integration -q`\n- Agents checks from `agents/`:\n  - `uv sync`\n  - `uv run python scripts/smoke_test.py`\n- Frontend checks from `client/`:\n  - `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`\n  - `pnpm run lint`\n  - `pnpm run build`\n  - `pnpm exec playwright install`\n  - `pnpm run test:e2e`\n- Local startup today:\n  - The agents service runs behind a lightweight proxy that enforces the `X-Internal-Token` shared secret. The backend calls the proxy on port 8020, and the proxy forwards to the ADK server on port 8021. `/health` stays public for simple availability checks.\n  - `docker-compose up -d db`\n  - `cd server && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=your-internal-token-here && uv run alembic upgrade head && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=your-internal-token-here && uv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8021 .`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=your-internal-token-here && uv run uvicorn app.proxy:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020`\n  - `cd agents && uv run adk web --port 8001` (optional UI)\n  - `cd client && pnpm install && pnpm run dev`\n- When expanding `agents/`, keep startup and validation commands updated in this file.\n\n## Current Coverage And Next-Step Expectations\n\n### Implemented (✅)\n- **Backend:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout/me with JWT + httponly cookies), health checks, database schema (10+ models for weather/AQI/flooding/outages/traffic, sessions, conversation history), `/api/chat` endpoint with full `RiskCardPayload` and `ArtifactPayload` response schema\n- **Backend Internal Routes:** All `/internal/*` data routes (weather, AQI, lakes, floods, outages, traffic, ward profile, locations) — token-gated, consumed only by agent tools\n- **Backend Services:** `data_service.py` (all domain DB queries + neighbourhood→zone mapping), `conversation_service.py` (session upsert + turn append to `ConversationHistory`), `agent_proxy.py` (Level-2 and Level-1 ADK response parsing)\n- **Backend-to-Agent:** Proxy service with `X-Internal-Token` validation, session routing to ADK\n- **Agent Subagent Hierarchy:** Five-agent system (`root_agent → chat_agent → flood_vulnerability_agent / heat_health_agent / infrastructure_agent / logistics_agent`) using `gemini-2.5-flash`\n- **Agent Tools:** `flood_tools` (lake hydrology, flood incidents), `heat_tools` (AQI current/historical, ward profile), `infra_tools` (power outage events), `logistics_tools` (traffic current/corridor), `shared/weather_tools` (weather current/historical), `shared/location_tools` (resolve/list locations), `shared/document_tools` (list indexes, fetch section)\n- **Orchestration:** `routing.py` (keyword-based intent classification: flood/heat/infra/logistics/greeting), `response_contract.py` (`build_level2_response()` structured output builder)\n- **Prompts:** Domain-specific system prompts for all five agents; `CHAT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION` mandates JSON-only structured output with data freshness caveats and off-domain refusal\n- **Data Loader Script:** `server/scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py` — reads 6 CSVs and inserts reference + domain tables; run with `uv run python scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py --replace` (not yet executed)\n- **Frontend:** Chat UI (MessageBubble, MessageList, InputBar, ThinkingIndicator, SuggestedPrompts), auth context (login/signup/logout), RiskCard and ArtifactRenderer components, health polling\n- **Tests:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout/me), password hashing, health checks, basic chat endpoint\n\n### Planned (📋)\n- **Data Seeding:** `load_metrosense_dataset.py` exists and is ready to run; CSVs not yet loaded into PostgreSQL — run once before agent tools can return real data\n- **Document Ingestion:** Reference documents (`Documents_Metrosense/`) are read directly from the filesystem by `document_tools`; no DB ingestion needed\n- **Streaming:** Responses are buffered POST/response; SSE or WebSocket streaming not yet implemented\n- **Message Context Loading:** `ConversationHistory` model and `conversation_service` exist; conversation context not yet injected into agent session on new requests\n- **Structured Risk Card Generation:** End-to-end schema is wired (`RiskCardPayload` in routes, `build_level2_response()` in agents); requires data seeding + agent logic to populate fields\n- **E2E Tests:** Playwright configured; no test suites written yet\n- **Chat Response Tests:** Coverage for agent output parsing, risk card generation, artifact rendering\n\n### Current Test Coverage\n- Backend: Health, auth (signup/login/logout/me), password hashing, basic chat routing\n- Frontend: Component renders (no E2E tests written yet)\n- Agents: smoke_test.py validates proxy routing only (not domain logic)\n\n### Next Steps (Recommended Order)\n1. **Run data loader** — `cd server && uv run python scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py --replace` to seed CSVs into PostgreSQL so agent tools return real data\n2. **Validate agent tool responses** — Smoke-test each domain tool end-to-end (internal routes → data_service → PostgreSQL) after seeding\n3. **Wire conversation context** — Pass recent `ConversationHistory` turns to the agent session so the agent has chat memory\n4. **Validate structured output** — Confirm `build_level2_response()` + `RiskCardPayload` round-trip produces renderable frontend cards\n5. **Implement streaming** — Add SSE or WebSocket to `/api/chat` for incremental text delivery\n6. **Write E2E tests** — Playwright suites covering login → chat → risk card render\n\n## Agent Guidance\n- Before major implementation work, verify repo truth from `README.md`, `server/pyproject.toml`, `client/package.json`, and `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`.\n- Prefer `rg` for search.\n- Do not document or reference unimplemented services as if they already exist.\n- **Important:** The CSV data files (`DataSet_MetroSense/`) exist in the repo and the loader script (`server/scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py`) is implemented but **not yet run**. Seed the database before expecting agent domain tools to return real data.\n- Documents (`Documents_Metrosense/`) are served directly from the filesystem by `document_tools` — no DB load needed, but `DOCUMENTS_PATH` env must point to the correct directory.\n- The `metrosense_agent/` is the production agent; `metrosearch_agent/` is a legacy skeleton that can be removed. All five subagents and their domain tools are implemented and wired into the ADK hierarchy.\n- The `response_contract.py` `build_level2_response()` builder and all Pydantic schemas (`RiskCardPayload`, `ArtifactPayload`) are in place end-to-end — the remaining gap is data availability and agent logic populating those fields.\n- If current behavior and MetroSense direction diverge, align new work to the MetroSense direction while keeping transitions explicit and incremental.\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- google-adk-python: Expert guidance on the Google Agent Development Kit (ADK) for Python. Use this skill when the user asks about building agents, using tools, streaming, callbacks, tutorials, deployment, or advanced architecture with the Google ADK in Python. (file: /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/.agents/skills/google-adk-python/SKILL.md)\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}
```

## Entry 187
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T18:30:36.047Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:30:36.047Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_message",
    "message": "Fix it at three layers, not one.\n\n**1. Fix the agent prompt**\nThe chat prompt in [chat.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/prompts/chat.py) needs an explicit rule for comparison/table requests:\n\n- If user asks for “table”, “compare”, “comparison”, “vs”, “month-to-month”, emit:\n  - concise `response_text`\n  - an `artifact` with tabular data\n- Never dump raw `details: {...}` or `summary:` labels into `response_text`\n- Keep optional advisory content brief and subordinate to the requested format\n\nRight now the prompt is too focused on prose + risk cards, so the model falls back to semi-structured text.\n\n**2. Add backend normalization/validation for artifacts**\nIn [agent_proxy.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/app/services/agent_proxy.py), normalize common table-like outputs before they hit the API model.\n\nPractical approach:\n- If parsed JSON contains `details` with comparable month blocks, convert it into canonical `artifact`\n- If `artifact.type == \"table\"`, validate rows/columns shape\n- Strip leaked keys like `summary:` / `details:` from user-facing text\n\nThat gives you a compatibility layer even when the model is imperfect.\n\n**3. Strengthen the response schema**\nThe current artifact model in [chat.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/app/api/routes/chat.py) is too generic:\n- `type`\n- `title`\n- `source`\n- `description`\n\nThat is not enough for real tables. Add a structured table artifact, for example:\n- `type: \"table\"`\n- `title`\n- `columns: list[str]`\n- `rows: list[list[str | int | float | null]]`\n- `description`\n\nThen render it cleanly in the frontend instead of stuffing HTML or dicts into `source`.\n\n**4. Update frontend rendering**\nIn the client, [ArtifactRenderer](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/client/src/components/chat/ArtifactRenderer.tsx) should support a native table artifact. That lets the answer render as an actual comparison table instead of raw JSON-ish text.\n\n**5. Add tests for this exact failure mode**\nAdd backend tests covering:\n- “compare September and October 2025 in Bellandur”\n- request containing “table”\n- agent returns legacy `summary/details/health_advisory`\n- backend normalizes to canonical `response_text + artifact`\n\nBest test locations:\n- [test_agent_proxy.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/tests/unit/test_agent_proxy.py)\n- [test_chat_api.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/tests/unit/test_chat_api.py)\n\n**Minimum viable fix**\nIf you want the fastest safe fix:\n1. Update the prompt to forbid raw dict dumping.\n2. Add backend normalization for table-like comparison outputs.\n3. Add a proper table artifact schema.\n4. Render that artifact in the client.\n\nThat will solve the exact Bellandur response you showed and prevent similar regressions. If you want, I can implement that end-to-end now.",
    "phase": "final_answer"
  }
}
```

    "message": "Fix it at three layers, not one.\n\n**1. Fix the agent prompt**\nThe chat prompt in [chat.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/prompts/chat.py) needs an explicit rule for comparison/table requests:\n\n- If user asks for “table”, “compare”, “comparison”, “vs”, “month-to-month”, emit:\n  - concise `response_text`\n  - an `artifact` with tabular data\n- Never dump raw `details: {...}` or `summary:` labels into `response_text`\n- Keep optional advisory content brief and subordinate to the requested format\n\nRight now the prompt is too focused on prose + risk cards, so the model falls back to semi-structured text.\n\n**2. Add backend normalization/validation for artifacts**\nIn [agent_proxy.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/app/services/agent_proxy.py), normalize common table-like outputs before they hit the API model.\n\nPractical approach:\n- If parsed JSON contains `details` with comparable month blocks, convert it into canonical `artifact`\n- If `artifact.type == \"table\"`, validate rows/columns shape\n- Strip leaked keys like `summary:` / `details:` from user-facing text\n\nThat gives you a compatibility layer even when the model is imperfect.\n\n**3. Strengthen the response schema**\nThe current artifact model in [chat.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/app/api/routes/chat.py) is too generic:\n- `type`\n- `title`\n- `source`\n- `description`\n\nThat is not enough for real tables. Add a structured table artifact, for example:\n- `type: \"table\"`\n- `title`\n- `columns: list[str]`\n- `rows: list[list[str | int | float | null]]`\n- `description`\n\nThen render it cleanly in the frontend instead of stuffing HTML or dicts into `source`.\n\n**4. Update frontend rendering**\nIn the client, [ArtifactRenderer](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/client/src/components/chat/ArtifactRenderer.tsx) should support a native table artifact. That lets the answer render as an actual comparison table instead of raw JSON-ish text.\n\n**5. Add tests for this exact failure mode**\nAdd backend tests covering:\n- “compare September and October 2025 in Bellandur”\n- request containing “table”\n- agent returns legacy `summary/details/health_advisory`\n- backend normalizes to canonical `response_text + artifact`\n\nBest test locations:\n- [test_agent_proxy.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/tests/unit/test_agent_proxy.py)\n- [test_chat_api.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/tests/unit/test_chat_api.py)\n\n**Minimum viable fix**\nIf you want the fastest safe fix:\n1. Update the prompt to forbid raw dict dumping.\n2. Add backend normalization for table-like comparison outputs.\n3. Add a proper table artifact schema.\n4. Render that artifact in the client.\n\nThat will solve the exact Bellandur response you showed and prevent similar regressions. If you want, I can implement that end-to-end now.",
    "phase": "final_answer"
  }
}
```

## Entry 192

**Role/Type:** turn_context
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:31:20.000Z",
  "type": "turn_context",
  "payload": {
    "turn_id": "019cde2a-e8a7-7ac3-8476-bc0435abed05",
    "cwd": "/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense",
    "current_date": "2026-03-12",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "writable_roots": [
        "/home/dell/.codex/memories"
      ],
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.4",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "default",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.4",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\n\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\n\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\n\n## request_user_input availability\n\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\n\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\n"
      }
    },
    "realtime_active": false,
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "none",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Project Identity\n- This repository is the working MetroSense codebase: `client/` for React, `server/` for FastAPI, PostgreSQL via Docker Compose, and CI/quality gates.\n- The product is `MetroSense`, a chat-based climate and infrastructure intelligence system for Bengaluru.\n- The target architecture is a three-service system:\n  - `client/`: frontend chat application\n  - `server/`: public backend API and orchestration layer\n  - `agents/`: internal agent server and tool-execution layer (✅ **exists and wired**, current status: skeleton with google_search tool only)\n- Treat the current implementation as the baseline and evolve it toward this three-service design instead of creating parallel app structures.\n- **Status**: ~70% complete. Auth flow, backend-to-agent routing, database schema, internal data routes, custom domain agent tools, structured response contract, and full subagent hierarchy are functional. Data seeding (script ready, not yet run), streaming, and E2E tests remain.\n\n## Canonical Architecture\n- The intended request flow is:\n  - `frontend -> backend -> agent`\n- The frontend should never call the agent service directly.\n- `server/` is the only public API boundary for browser clients.\n- `agents/` is an internal service boundary responsible for agent runtime behavior, tool usage, and structured chat output generation.\n- If existing docs still use `agent/` singular, treat that as referring to the planned `agents/` subsystem until naming is aligned across the repo.\n\n## Why This Boundary Exists\n- Keep secrets, database access, internal tools, and agent infrastructure off the public internet.\n- Let the backend own request validation, session coordination, auth, rate limiting, logging, and response shaping.\n- Preserve a stable frontend contract even if the agent framework, prompting strategy, or toolchain changes.\n- Keep observability and failure handling centralized in the backend rather than split between browser and agent server.\n\n## Tech Stack\n- Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic, PostgreSQL 16, Loguru, `uv`\n- Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite 5, MUI v6, React Query, Zustand, Axios, `pnpm`\n- Tooling: Ruff, MyPy strict, import-linter, ESLint, Docker, Docker Compose, GitHub Actions\n\n## Repository Shape\n- `client/`\n  - Chat UI, streaming presentation, structured response rendering, browser-facing state management.\n- `server/`\n  - Public HTTP API, request validation, orchestration, session handling, backend-to-agent coordination.\n- `agents/`\n  - Internal agent service for chat generation, tool calls, retrieval, and domain intelligence.\n- `server/app/api/`\n  - FastAPI routes and request/response boundaries.\n- `server/app/services/`\n  - Backend business logic and internal orchestration code.\n- `server/app/db/`\n  - Database models, metadata, and session management.\n- `server/tests/unit/`\n  - Fast isolated tests for backend logic and simple route behavior.\n- `server/tests/integration/`\n  - App-level and database-backed tests.\n- `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`\n  - Source of truth for the planned MetroSense chat UX and structured response behavior.\n\n## Architectural Rules\n- Preserve backend layering:\n  - `app.api` may depend on `app.services` and `app.core`\n  - `app.services` may depend on `app.db` and `app.core`\n  - `app.db` must not depend on `app.services` or `app.api`\n- Keep the frontend focused on rendering and interaction, not business orchestration.\n- Route all browser chat traffic through `server/`, even after `agents/` exists.\n- Treat backend-to-agent communication as an internal interface that may evolve without breaking the UI.\n- Prefer extending existing `server/` and `client/` entrypoints over introducing duplicate service layers or alternative public APIs.\n\n## Authentication\n- Public endpoints:\n  - `POST /api/auth/signup`\n  - `POST /api/auth/login`\n- Protected endpoints:\n  - All other `/api/*` routes require a valid JWT cookie.\n- `/health` remains public for uptime checks.\n\n## Product Direction\n- MetroSense is intended to be a chat-first application for weather risk, flooding, AQI, outage, traffic, and infrastructure-readiness questions.\n- Expected response modes:\n  - streamed assistant text for most responses\n  - agent-emitted `RiskCard` payloads for structured neighborhood risk outputs\n  - agent-emitted `Artifact` payloads for charts, maps, tables, or other visual responses\n- Structured outputs should be decided by the agent layer and delivered through the backend to the frontend.\n- The frontend must render structured responses based on explicit backend/agent event types, not keyword inference.\n\n## Testing And Validation\n- Backend checks from `server/`:\n  - `uv sync --all-extras`\n  - `uv run ruff format --check .`\n  - `uv run ruff check .`\n  - `uv run mypy .`\n  - `uv run lint-imports`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/unit -q`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/integration -q`\n- Agents checks from `agents/`:\n  - `uv sync`\n  - `uv run python scripts/smoke_test.py`\n- Frontend checks from `client/`:\n  - `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`\n  - `pnpm run lint`\n  - `pnpm run build`\n  - `pnpm exec playwright install`\n  - `pnpm run test:e2e`\n- Local startup today:\n  - The agents service runs behind a lightweight proxy that enforces the `X-Internal-Token` shared secret. The backend calls the proxy on port 8020, and the proxy forwards to the ADK server on port 8021. `/health` stays public for simple availability checks.\n  - `docker-compose up -d db`\n  - `cd server && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=your-internal-token-here && uv run alembic upgrade head && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=your-internal-token-here && uv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8021 .`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=your-internal-token-here && uv run uvicorn app.proxy:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020`\n  - `cd agents && uv run adk web --port 8001` (optional UI)\n  - `cd client && pnpm install && pnpm run dev`\n- When expanding `agents/`, keep startup and validation commands updated in this file.\n\n## Current Coverage And Next-Step Expectations\n\n### Implemented (✅)\n- **Backend:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout/me with JWT + httponly cookies), health checks, database schema (10+ models for weather/AQI/flooding/outages/traffic, sessions, conversation history), `/api/chat` endpoint with full `RiskCardPayload` and `ArtifactPayload` response schema\n- **Backend Internal Routes:** All `/internal/*` data routes (weather, AQI, lakes, floods, outages, traffic, ward profile, locations) — token-gated, consumed only by agent tools\n- **Backend Services:** `data_service.py` (all domain DB queries + neighbourhood→zone mapping), `conversation_service.py` (session upsert + turn append to `ConversationHistory`), `agent_proxy.py` (Level-2 and Level-1 ADK response parsing)\n- **Backend-to-Agent:** Proxy service with `X-Internal-Token` validation, session routing to ADK\n- **Agent Subagent Hierarchy:** Five-agent system (`root_agent → chat_agent → flood_vulnerability_agent / heat_health_agent / infrastructure_agent / logistics_agent`) using `gemini-2.5-flash`\n- **Agent Tools:** `flood_tools` (lake hydrology, flood incidents), `heat_tools` (AQI current/historical, ward profile), `infra_tools` (power outage events), `logistics_tools` (traffic current/corridor), `shared/weather_tools` (weather current/historical), `shared/location_tools` (resolve/list locations), `shared/document_tools` (list indexes, fetch section)\n- **Orchestration:** `routing.py` (keyword-based intent classification: flood/heat/infra/logistics/greeting), `response_contract.py` (`build_level2_response()` structured output builder)\n- **Prompts:** Domain-specific system prompts for all five agents; `CHAT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION` mandates JSON-only structured output with data freshness caveats and off-domain refusal\n- **Data Loader Script:** `server/scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py` — reads 6 CSVs and inserts reference + domain tables; run with `uv run python scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py --replace` (not yet executed)\n- **Frontend:** Chat UI (MessageBubble, MessageList, InputBar, ThinkingIndicator, SuggestedPrompts), auth context (login/signup/logout), RiskCard and ArtifactRenderer components, health polling\n- **Tests:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout/me), password hashing, health checks, basic chat endpoint\n\n### Planned (📋)\n- **Data Seeding:** `load_metrosense_dataset.py` exists and is ready to run; CSVs not yet loaded into PostgreSQL — run once before agent tools can return real data\n- **Document Ingestion:** Reference documents (`Documents_Metrosense/`) are read directly from the filesystem by `document_tools`; no DB ingestion needed\n- **Streaming:** Responses are buffered POST/response; SSE or WebSocket streaming not yet implemented\n- **Message Context Loading:** `ConversationHistory` model and `conversation_service` exist; conversation context not yet injected into agent session on new requests\n- **Structured Risk Card Generation:** End-to-end schema is wired (`RiskCardPayload` in routes, `build_level2_response()` in agents); requires data seeding + agent logic to populate fields\n- **E2E Tests:** Playwright configured; no test suites written yet\n- **Chat Response Tests:** Coverage for agent output parsing, risk card generation, artifact rendering\n\n### Current Test Coverage\n- Backend: Health, auth (signup/login/logout/me), password hashing, basic chat routing\n- Frontend: Component renders (no E2E tests written yet)\n- Agents: smoke_test.py validates proxy routing only (not domain logic)\n\n### Next Steps (Recommended Order)\n1. **Run data loader** — `cd server && uv run python scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py --replace` to seed CSVs into PostgreSQL so agent tools return real data\n2. **Validate agent tool responses** — Smoke-test each domain tool end-to-end (internal routes → data_service → PostgreSQL) after seeding\n3. **Wire conversation context** — Pass recent `ConversationHistory` turns to the agent session so the agent has chat memory\n4. **Validate structured output** — Confirm `build_level2_response()` + `RiskCardPayload` round-trip produces renderable frontend cards\n5. **Implement streaming** — Add SSE or WebSocket to `/api/chat` for incremental text delivery\n6. **Write E2E tests** — Playwright suites covering login → chat → risk card render\n\n## Agent Guidance\n- Before major implementation work, verify repo truth from `README.md`, `server/pyproject.toml`, Seed the database before expecting agent domain tools to return real data.\n- Documents (`Documents_Metrosense/`) are served directly from the filesystem by `document_tools` — no DB load needed, but `DOCUMENTS_PATH` env must point to the correct directory.\n- The `metrosense_agent/` is the production agent; `metrosearch_agent/` is a legacy skeleton that can be removed.     "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Project Identity\n- This repository is the working MetroSense codebase: `client/` for React, `server/` for FastAPI, PostgreSQL via Docker Compose, and CI/quality gates.\n- The product is `MetroSense`, a chat-based climate and infrastructure intelligence system for Bengaluru.\n- The target architecture is a three-service system:\n  - `client/`: frontend chat application\n  - `server/`: public backend API and orchestration layer\n  - `agents/`: internal agent server and tool-execution layer (✅ **exists and wired**, current status: skeleton with google_search tool only)\n- Treat the current implementation as the baseline and evolve it toward this three-service design instead of creating parallel app structures.\n- **Status**: ~70% complete. Auth flow, backend-to-agent routing, database schema, internal data routes, custom domain agent tools, structured response contract, and full subagent hierarchy are functional. Data seeding (script ready, not yet run), streaming, and E2E tests remain.\n\n## Canonical Architecture\n- The intended request flow is:\n  - `frontend -> backend -> agent`\n- The frontend should never call the agent service directly.\n- `server/` is the only public API boundary for browser clients.\n- `agents/` is an internal service boundary responsible for agent runtime behavior, tool usage, and structured chat output generation.\n- If existing docs still use `agent/` singular, treat that as referring to the planned `agents/` subsystem until naming is aligned across the repo.\n\n## Why This Boundary Exists\n- Keep secrets, database access, internal tools, and agent infrastructure off the public internet.\n- Let the backend own request validation, session coordination, auth, rate limiting, logging, and response shaping.\n- Preserve a stable frontend contract even if the agent framework, prompting strategy, or toolchain changes.\n- Keep observability and failure handling centralized in the backend rather than split between browser and agent server.\n\n## Tech Stack\n- Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic, PostgreSQL 16, Loguru, `uv`\n- Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite 5, MUI v6, React Query, Zustand, Axios, `pnpm`\n- Tooling: Ruff, MyPy strict, import-linter, ESLint, Docker, Docker Compose, GitHub Actions\n\n## Repository Shape\n- `client/`\n  - Chat UI, streaming presentation, structured response rendering, browser-facing state management.\n- `server/`\n  - Public HTTP API, request validation, orchestration, session handling, backend-to-agent coordination.\n- `agents/`\n  - Internal agent service for chat generation, tool calls, retrieval, and domain intelligence.\n- `server/app/api/`\n  - FastAPI routes and request/response boundaries.\n- `server/app/services/`\n  - Backend business logic and internal orchestration code.\n- `server/app/db/`\n  - Database models, metadata, and session management.\n- `server/tests/unit/`\n  - Fast isolated tests for backend logic and simple route behavior.\n- `server/tests/integration/`\n  - App-level and database-backed tests.\n- `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`\n  - Source of truth for the planned MetroSense chat UX and structured response behavior.\n\n## Architectural Rules\n- Preserve backend layering:\n  - `app.api` may depend on `app.services` and `app.core`\n  - `app.services` may depend on `app.db` and `app.core`\n  - `app.db` must not depend on `app.services` or `app.api`\n- Keep the frontend focused on rendering and interaction, not business orchestration.\n- Route all browser chat traffic through `server/`, even after `agents/` exists.\n- Treat backend-to-agent communication as an internal interface that may evolve without breaking the UI.\n- Prefer extending existing `server/` and `client/` entrypoints over introducing duplicate service layers or alternative public APIs.\n\n## Authentication\n- Public endpoints:\n  - `POST /api/auth/signup`\n  - `POST /api/auth/login`\n- Protected endpoints:\n  - All other `/api/*` routes require a valid JWT cookie.\n- `/health` remains public for uptime checks.\n\n## Product Direction\n- MetroSense is intended to be a chat-first application for weather risk, flooding, AQI, outage, traffic, and infrastructure-readiness questions.\n- Expected response modes:\n  - streamed assistant text for most responses\n  - agent-emitted `RiskCard` payloads for structured neighborhood risk outputs\n  - agent-emitted `Artifact` payloads for charts, maps, tables, or other visual responses\n- Structured outputs should be decided by the agent layer and delivered through the backend to the frontend.\n- The frontend must render structured responses based on explicit backend/agent event types, not keyword inference.\n\n## Testing And Validation\n- Backend checks from `server/`:\n  - `uv sync --all-extras`\n  - `uv run ruff format --check .`\n  - `uv run ruff check .`\n  - `uv run mypy .`\n  - `uv run lint-imports`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/unit -q`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/integration -q`\n- Agents checks from `agents/`:\n  - `uv sync`\n  - `uv run python scripts/smoke_test.py`\n- Frontend checks from `client/`:\n  - `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`\n  - `pnpm run lint`\n  - `pnpm run build`\n  - `pnpm exec playwright install`\n  - `pnpm run test:e2e`\n- Local startup today:\n  - The agents service runs behind a lightweight proxy that enforces the `X-Internal-Token` shared secret. The backend calls the proxy on port 8020, and the proxy forwards to the ADK server on port 8021. `/health` stays public for simple availability checks.\n  - `docker-compose up -d db`\n  - `cd server && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=your-internal-token-here && uv run alembic upgrade head && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010`\n  - `cd agents && export app.proxy:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020`\n  - `cd agents && uv run adk web --port 8001` (optional UI)\n  - `cd client && pnpm install && pnpm run dev`\n- When expanding `agents/`, keep startup and   }
}

last_active_at: string;\n  messages: ChatTranscriptMessage[];\n}\n\nexport interface HealthResponse {\n  backend: \"ok\" | \"down\";\n  agent: \"ok\" | \"down\";\n  status: AgentStatus;\n}\n\nexport interface AppError {\n  code: string;\n  message: string;\n}\n\nexport interface Message {\n  id: string;\n  role: \"user\" | \"assistant\";\n  content: string;\n  timestamp: Date;\n  riskCard?: RiskCardPayload;\n  artifact?: ArtifactPayload;\n  dataFreshnessSummary?: Record<string, unknown>;\n  followUpPrompt?: string;\n  isError?: boolean;\n}\n"

## Entry 204
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T18:31:27.237Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:31:27.237Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_swMp0Daebbp39sVeUmC5qcjs",
    "output": "Chunk ID: bc087b\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 649\nOutput:\nimport { useMemo, useState } from \"react\";\nimport OpenInFullRoundedIcon from \"@mui/icons-material/OpenInFullRounded\";\nimport WarningAmberRoundedIcon from \"@mui/icons-material/WarningAmberRounded\";\nimport { Box, Dialog, DialogContent, DialogTitle, IconButton, Skeleton, Stack, Typography } from \"@mui/material\";\nimport { buildArtifactSrcDoc, sanitizeArtifactHtml } from \"@/lib/artifactSandbox\";\nimport type { ArtifactPayload } from \"@/types/chat\";\n\ninterface ArtifactRendererProps {\n  artifact: ArtifactPayload;\n}\n\nexport default function ArtifactRenderer({ artifact }: ArtifactRendererProps) {\n  const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);\n\n  const sanitized = useMemo(() => sanitizeArtifactHtml(artifact.source), [artifact.source]);\n  const srcDoc = useMemo(() => buildArtifactSrcDoc(sanitized, artifact.title), [sanitized, artifact.title]);\n  const hasRenderableContent = sanitized.length > 0;\n\n  const frame = hasRenderableContent ? (\n    <Box\n      component=\"iframe\"\n      title={artifact.title}\n      srcDoc={srcDoc}\n      sandbox=\"\"\n      sx={{ width: \"100%\", height: 280, border: 0, display: \"block\", backgroundColor: \"#fff\" }}\n    />\n  ) : (\n    <Stack direction=\"row\" spacing={1} alignItems=\"flex-start\" sx={{ border: \"1px solid #F2C7CE\", borderRadius: 2, backgroundColor: \"#FFF0F2\", p: 2 }}>\n      <WarningAmberRoundedIcon color=\"error\" />\n      <Box>\n        <Typography fontWeight={700}>Chart could not be rendered</Typography>\n        <Typography color=\"text.secondary\">{artifact.description ?? \"The artifact did not contain safe renderable HTML.\"}</Typography>\n      </Box>\n    </Stack>\n  );\n\n  return (\n    <Box role=\"figure\" aria-label={artifact.title} sx={{ border: \"1px solid #D1DBE8\", borderRadius: 2, overflow: \"hidden\", backgroundColor: \"#fff\" }}>\n      <Stack direction=\"row\" alignItems=\"center\" justifyContent=\"space-between\" sx={{ px: 2, py: 1.25, backgroundColor: \"#F7F9FC\", borderBottom: \"1px solid #D1DBE8\" }}>\n        <Typography fontSize={13} fontWeight={600}>\n          {artifact.title}\n        </Typography>\n        <IconButton aria-label={`Expand chart: ${artifact.title}`} onClick={() => setOpen(true)}>\n          <OpenInFullRoundedIcon fontSize=\"small\" />\n        </IconButton>\n      </Stack>\n      <Box sx={{ p: 2, minHeight: 200 }}>\n        {artifact.source ? frame : <Skeleton variant=\"rounded\" height={220} />}\n      </Box>\n      <Dialog open={open} onClose={() => setOpen(false)} fullWidth maxWidth=\"lg\" aria-modal=\"true\">\n        <DialogTitle>{artifact.title}</DialogTitle>\n        <DialogContent>{frame}</DialogContent>\n      </Dialog>\n    </Box>\n  );\n}\n"
  }
}
```

{

  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_message",
    "message": "I have the client surface now. The current artifact model only supports HTML, so I need to widen the schema and renderer for a native table payload instead of forcing comparison data through `source` HTML. After that I’ll patch the backend parser to detect and convert the `summary/details` weather-comparison shape you showed.",
    "phase": "commentary"
  }
}
```

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:31:33.070Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_YhDCNhNcux3C3hOXQAewAq4U",
    "output": "Chunk ID: be5e33\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 1619\nOutput:\nimport { useCallback, useEffect, useMemo, useRef, useState } from \"react\";\nimport { AxiosError } from \"axios\";\nimport { getChatSession, getChatSessions, getHealth, postChat } from \"@/lib/api\";\nimport type {\n  AgentStatus,\n  AppError,\n  ArtifactPayload,\n  ChatSessionSummary,\n  ChatTranscriptMessage,\n  Message,\n  RiskCardPayload,\n} from \"@/types/chat\";\n\nconst HEALTH_POLL_MS = 30_000;\nconst PROMPTS = [\n  \"If it rains 60mm tonight, which underpasses should be barricaded?\",\n  \"How does current humidity compare to pre-monsoon averages of the last decade?\",\n  \"Which logistics routes through Bengaluru are highest risk today?\",\n  \"Generate a risk scorecard for Sarjapur Road.\",\n];\n\nfunction createSessionId(): string {\n  if (typeof crypto !== \"undefined\" && \"randomUUID\" in crypto) {\n    return crypto.randomUUID();\n  }\n  return `session-${Date.now()}`;\n}\n\nfunction createMessage(partial: Omit<Message, \"id\" | \"timestamp\">): Message {\n  return {\n    id: `${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(16).slice(2)}`,\n    timestamp: new Date(),\n    ...partial,\n  };\n}\n\nfunction extractError(error: unknown): AppError {\n  if (error instanceof AxiosError) {\n    const payload = error.response?.data as { error?: AppError } | undefined;\n    if (payload?.error) {\n      return payload.error;\n    }\n  }\n  return {\n    code: \"UNKNOWN_ERROR\",\n    message: \"Something went wrong while contacting MetroSense.\",\n  };\n}\n\nexport interface UseChatResult {\n  agentStatus: AgentStatus;\n  error: AppError | null;\n  isHistoryLoading: boolean;\n  input: string;\n  isSending: boolean;\n  mode: \"live\" | \"history_readonly\";\n  messages: Message[];\n  prompts: string[];\n  selectedHistorySessionId: string | null;\n  sessionId: string;\n  sessions: ChatSessionSummary[];\n  createNewChat: () => void;\n  loadHistorySession: (sessionId: string) => Promise<void>;\n  setInput: (value: string) => void;\n  sendMessage: (value?: string) => Promise<void>;\n}\n\nfunction fromTranscriptMessage(message: ChatTranscriptMessage): Message {\n  return {\n    id: `${message.timestamp}-${Math.random().toString(16).slice(2)}`,\n    role: message.role,\n    content: message.message,\n    timestamp: new Date(message.timestamp),\n  };\n}\n\nexport function useChat(): UseChatResult {\n  const [messages, setMessages] = useState<Message[]>([]);\n  const [input, setInput] = useState(\"\");\n  const [isSending, setIsSending] = useState(false);\n  const [isHistoryLoading, setIsHistoryLoading] = useState(false);\n  const [agentStatus, setAgentStatus] = useState<AgentStatus>(\"connecting\");\n  const [error, setError] = useState<AppError | null>(null);\n  const [sessions, setSessions] = useState<ChatSessionSummary[]>([]);\n  const [mode, setMode] = useState<\"live\" | \"history_readonly\">(\"live\");\n  const [selectedHistorySessionId, setSelectedHistorySessionId] = useState<string | null>(null);\n  const sessionIdRef = useRef<string>(createSessionId());\n\n  const refreshHealth = useCallback(async () => {\n    try {\n      const response = await getHealth();\n      setAgentStatus(response.status);\n    } catch {\n      setAgentStatus(\"degraded\");\n    }\n  }, []);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    void refreshHealth();\n    const interval = window.setInterval(() => {\n      void refreshHealth();\n    }, HEALTH_POLL_MS);\n    return () => window.clearInterval(interval);\n  }, [refreshHealth]);\n\n  const refreshSessions = useCallback(async () => {\n    try {\n      const response = await getChatSessions();\n      setSessions(response.sessions);\n    } catch {\n      // Keep chat usable if history fetch fails.\n    }\n  }, []);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    void refreshSessions();\n  }, [refreshSessions]);\n\n  const createNewChat = useCallback(() => {\n    setMode(\"live\");\n    setSelectedHistorySessionId(null);\n    sessionIdRef.current = createSessionId();\n    setMessages([]);\n    setInput(\"\");\n    setError(null);\n  }, []);\n\n  const loadHistorySession = useCallback(async (sessionId: string) => {\n    setError(null);\n    setIsHistoryLoading(true);\n    try {\n      const transcript = await getChatSession(sessionId);\n      setMessages(transcript.messages.map(fromTranscriptMessage));\n      setMode(\"history_readonly\");\n      setSelectedHistorySessionId(transcript.session_id);\n    } catch (caughtError) {\n      setError(extractError(caughtError));\n    } finally {\n      setIsHistoryLoading(false);\n    }\n  }, []);\n\n  const sendMessage = useCallback(\n    async (value?: string) => {\n      const messageText = (value ?? input).trim();\n      if (mode !== \"live\" || !messageText || isSending) {\n        return;\n      }\n\n      setError(null);\n      setIsSending(true);\n      setMessages((current) => [\n        ...current,\n        createMessage({ role: \"user\", content: messageText }),\n      ]);\n      setInput(\"\");\n\n      try {\n        const response = await postChat({\n          session_id: sessionIdRef.current,\n          message: messageText,\n        });\n\n        const assistantMessage = createMessage({\n          role: \"assistant\",\n          content: response.response_text ?? response.message,\n          riskCard: response.risk_card ?? undefined,\n          artifact: response.artifact ?? undefined,\n          dataFreshnessSummary: response.data_freshness_summary ?? undefined,\n          followUpPrompt: response.follow_up_prompt ?? undefined,\n        });\n\n        setMessages((current) => [...current, assistantMessage]);\n        await refreshSessions();\n      } catch (caughtError) {\n        const appError = extractError(caughtError);\n        setError(appError);\n        setMessages((current) => [\n          ...current,\n          createMessage({\n            role: \"assistant\",\n            content: appError.message,\n            isError: true,\n          }),\n        ]);\n      } finally {\n        setIsSending(false);\n        void refreshHealth();\n      }\n    },\n    [input, isSending, mode, refreshHealth, refreshSessions],\n  );\n\n  return useMemo(\n    () => ({\n      agentStatus,\n      createNewChat,\n      error,\n      isHistoryLoading,\n      input,\n      isSending,\n      loadHistorySession,\n      mode,\n      messages,\n      prompts: PROMPTS,\n      selectedHistorySessionId,\n      sessionId: sessionIdRef.current,\n      sessions,\n      setInput,\n      sendMessage,\n    }),\n    [\n      agentStatus,\n      createNewChat,\n      error,\n      isHistoryLoading,\n      input,\n      isSending,\n      loadHistorySession,\n      mode,\n      messages,\n      selectedHistorySessionId,\n      sessions,\n      sendMessage,\n    ],\n  );\n}\n\nexport type { ArtifactPayload, RiskCardPayload };\n"
  }
}

    "output": "Chunk ID: fbe113\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 174\nOutput:\nimport { render, screen } from \"@testing-library/react\";\nimport { describe, expect, it } from \"vitest\";\nimport ArtifactRenderer from \"@/components/chat/ArtifactRenderer\";\n\ndescribe(\"ArtifactRenderer\", () => {\n  it(\"renders a fallback when sanitized artifact content is empty\", () => {\n    render(\n      <ArtifactRenderer\n        artifact={{\n          type: \"html\",\n          title: \"Unsafe chart\",\n          source: '<script>alert(1)</script>',\n          description: \"Unsafe chart description\",\n        }}\n      />,\n    );\n\n    expect(screen.getByText(\"Chart could not be rendered\")).toBeInTheDocument();\n    expect(screen.getByText(\"Unsafe chart description\")).toBeInTheDocument();\n  });\n});\n"
  }
}

## Entry 216
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T18:31:33.111Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:31:33.111Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_ETJ4mixLKzBoEFUIMRX9IxGg",
    "output": "Chunk ID: db4a0f\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 428\nOutput:\nimport { act, renderHook, waitFor } from \"@testing-library/react\";\nimport { beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from \"vitest\";\nimport { getChatSession, getChatSessions, getHealth, postChat } from \"@/lib/api\";\nimport { useChat } from \"@/hooks/useChat\";\n\nvi.mock(\"@/lib/api\", () => ({\n  getChatSession: vi.fn(),\n  getChatSessions: vi.fn(),\n  getHealth: vi.fn(),\n  postChat: vi.fn(),\n}));\n\ndescribe(\"useChat\", () => {\n  beforeEach(() => {\n    vi.mocked(getChatSession).mockResolvedValue({\n      session_id: \"s-1\",\n      title: \"Old chat\",\n      last_active_at: \"2026-03-10T12:00:00Z\",\n      messages: [],\n    });\n    vi.mocked(getChatSessions).mockResolvedValue({ sessions: [] });\n    vi.mocked(getHealth).mockResolvedValue({ backend: \"ok\", agent: \"ok\", status: \"online\" });\n  });\n\n  it(\"appends user and assistant messages on success\", async () => {\n    vi.mocked(postChat).mockResolvedValue({\n      response_text: \"Agent reply\",\n      message: \"Agent reply\",\n      risk_card: null,\n      artifact: null,\n    });\n\n    const { result } = renderHook(() => useChat());\n\n    await act(async () => {\n      await result.current.sendMessage(\"Hello\");\n    });\n\n    await waitFor(() => expect(result.current.messages).toHaveLength(2));\n    expect(result.current.messages[0]?.content).toBe(\"Hello\");\n    expect(result.current.messages[1]?.content).toBe(\"Agent reply\");\n    expect(result.current.agentStatus).toBe(\"online\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"falls back to degraded status when health polling fails\", async () => {\n    vi.mocked(getHealth).mockRejectedValueOnce(new Error(\"down\"));\n\n    const { result } = renderHook(() => useChat());\n\n    await waitFor(() => expect(result.current.agentStatus).toBe(\"degraded\"));\n  });\n});\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 218
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T18:31:50.229Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:31:50.229Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_message",
    "message": "I’ve identified the compatibility gap that matters for your Bellandur example: the agent can emit a non-JSON `summary/details/health_advisory` blob, and the current parser just passes it through as plain text. I’m adding a legacy parser for that exact pattern, plus a first-class table artifact schema so comparison requests render properly in the UI instead of leaking internal dicts.",
    "phase": "commentary"
  }
}
```
**Metadata:**

I’m adding a legacy parser for that exact pattern, plus a first-class table artifact schema so comparison requests render properly in the UI instead of leaking internal dicts.",
    "phase": "commentary"
  }
}

## Entry 219

{

  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "message",
    "role": "assistant",
    "content": [
      {
        "type": "output_text",
        "text": "I’ve identified the compatibility gap that matters for your Bellandur example: the agent can emit a non-JSON `summary/details/health_advisory` blob, and the current parser just passes it through as plain text. I’m adding a legacy parser for that exact pattern, plus a first-class table artifact schema so comparison requests render properly in the UI instead of leaking internal dicts."
      }
    ],
    "phase": "commentary"
  }
}
```

## Entry 225
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T18:31:50.289Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:31:50.289Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_UUFmPv5F6bgDRDJ1cUcPLq3M",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 2aa505\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 460\nOutput:\nimport axios from \"axios\";\nimport type {\n  ChatRequest,\n  ChatResponse,\n  ChatSessionsResponse,\n  ChatTranscriptResponse,\n  HealthResponse,\n} from \"@/types/chat\";\n\nconst api = axios.create({\n  baseURL: import.meta.env.VITE_API_BASE_URL ?? \"/\",\n  withCredentials: true,\n});\n\nexport async function postChat(payload: ChatRequest): Promise<ChatResponse> {\n  const response = await api.post<ChatResponse>(\"/api/chat\", payload);\n  return response.data;\n}\n\nexport async function getHealth(): Promise<HealthResponse> {\n  const response = await api.get<HealthResponse>(\"/api/health\");\n  return response.data;\n}\n\nexport async function getChatSessions(limit = 20, offset = 0): Promise<ChatSessionsResponse> {\n  const response = await api.get<ChatSessionsResponse>(\"/api/chat/sessions\", { params: { limit, offset } });\n  return response.data;\n}\n\nexport async function getChatSession(sessionId: string): Promise<ChatTranscriptResponse> {\n  const response = await api.get<ChatTranscriptResponse>(`/api/chat/sessions/${sessionId}`);\n  return response.data;\n}\n\nexport interface AuthPayload {\n  email: string;\n  password: string;\n}\n\nexport interface UserResponse {\n  id: number;\n  email: string;\n}\n\nexport async function postSignup(payload: AuthPayload): Promise<UserResponse> {\n  const response = await api.post<{ user: UserResponse }>(\"/api/auth/signup\", payload);\n  return response.data.user;\n}\n\nexport async function postLogin(payload: AuthPayload): Promise<UserResponse> {\n  const response = await api.post<{ user: UserResponse }>(\"/api/auth/login\", payload);\n  return response.data.user;\n}\n\nexport async function postLogout(): Promise<void> {\n  await api.post(\"/api/auth/logout\");\n}\n\nexport async function getMe(): Promise<UserResponse> {\n  const response = await api.get<UserResponse>(\"/api/auth/me\");\n  return response.data;\n}\n\nexport default api;\n"
  }
}
```

{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:31:50.293Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_2pIPO0C78KKq1rExZCdhcHhT",
    "output": "Chunk ID: f50490\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 1687\nOutput:\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom datetime import UTC, datetime\n\nimport pytest\nfrom httpx import AsyncClient\n\nfrom app.api.deps import require_user\nfrom app.db.models import User\nfrom app.main import app\nfrom app.services import agent_proxy, conversation_service\n\n\n@pytest.fixture\ndef auth_override() -> None:\n    app.dependency_overrides[require_user] = lambda: User(\n        id=1,\n        email=\"test@example.com\",\n        password_hash=\"x\",\n    )\n    yield\n    app.dependency_overrides.pop(require_user, None)\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_chat_returns_json_payload(\n    client: AsyncClient, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch, auth_override: None\n) -> None:\n    async def _fake_chat_response(**_: object) -> dict[str, object]:\n        return {\n            \"session_id\": \"test-session\",\n            \"response_mode\": \"text\",\n            \"response_text\": \"Flood risk is elevated tonight.\",\n            \"citations_summary\": [],\n            \"data_freshness_summary\": {},\n            \"follow_up_prompt\": None,\n            \"message\": \"Flood risk is elevated tonight.\",\n            \"risk_card\": {\n                \"neighborhood\": \"SARJAPUR ROAD\",\n                \"generated_at\": \"2026-03-10T12:00:00Z\",\n                \"overall_risk_score\": 8.8,\n            },\n            \"artifact\": {\n                \"type\": \"html\",\n                \"title\": \"Rainfall Trend\",\n                \"source\": \"<div>chart</div>\",\n            },\n        }\n\n    monkeypatch.setattr(agent_proxy, \"get_chat_response\", _fake_chat_response)\n\n    response = await client.post(\n        \"/api/chat\",\n        json={\n            \"session_id\": \"test-session\",\n            \"message\": \"What is the flood risk tonight?\",\n        },\n    )\n\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    assert response.json() == {\n        \"session_id\": \"test-session\",\n        \"response_mode\": \"text\",\n        \"response_text\": \"Flood risk is elevated tonight.\",\n        \"citations_summary\": [],\n        \"data_freshness_summary\": {},\n        \"follow_up_prompt\": None,\n        \"message\": \"Flood risk is elevated tonight.\",\n        \"risk_card\": {\n            \"neighborhood\": \"SARJAPUR ROAD\",\n            \"generated_at\": \"2026-03-10T12:00:00Z\",\n            \"overall_risk_score\": 8.8,\n            \"flood_risk\": None,\n            \"power_outage_risk\": None,\n            \"traffic_delay_index\": None,\n            \"health_advisory\": None,\n            \"emergency_readiness\": None,\n            \"rainfall_expected_mm_per_hr\": None,\n            \"rainfall_classification\": None,\n            \"barricade_recommendations\": None,\n        },\n        \"artifact\": {\n            \"type\": \"html\",\n            \"title\": \"Rainfall Trend\",\n            \"source\": \"<div>chart</div>\",\n            \"description\": None,\n        },\n    }\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_chat_returns_agent_error_payload(\n    client: AsyncClient, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch, auth_override: None\n) -> None:\n    async def _raise_error(**_: object) -> dict[str, object]:\n        raise ValueError(\"bad payload\")\n\n    monkeypatch.setattr(agent_proxy, \"get_chat_response\", _raise_error)\n\n    response = await client.post(\n        \"/api/chat\",\n        json={\"session_id\": \"test-session\", \"message\": \"hello\"},\n    )\n\n    assert response.status_code == 502\n    assert response.json() == {\n        \"error\": {\n            \"code\": \"AGENT_UNAVAILABLE\",\n            \"message\": \"The agent service is currently unavailable.\",\n        }\n    }\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_api_health_degraded_when_agent_is_down(\n    client: AsyncClient, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch, auth_override: None\n) -> None:\n    async def _fake_health(_: object) -> dict[str, str]:\n        return {\"backend\": \"ok\", \"agent\": \"down\", \"status\": \"degraded\"}\n\n    monkeypatch.setattr(agent_proxy, \"get_agent_health\", _fake_health)\n\n    response = await client.get(\"/api/health\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    assert response.json() == {\n        \"backend\": \"ok\",\n        \"agent\": \"down\",\n        \"status\": \"degraded\",\n    }\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_list_chat_sessions_returns_summaries(\n    client: AsyncClient, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch, auth_override: None\n) -> None:\n    now = datetime(2026, 3, 10, 12, 0, tzinfo=UTC)\n    session_row = type(\n        \"SessionRow\",\n        (),\n        {\n            \"session_id\": \"s-1\",\n            \"title\": \"Bellandur flood risk\",\n            \"last_active_at\": now,\n            \"total_turns\": 6,\n        },\n    )()\n\n    async def _fake_list_sessions(**_: object) -> list[object]:\n        return [session_row]\n\n    monkeypatch.setattr(conversation_service, \"list_sessions\", _fake_list_sessions)\n\n    response = await client.get(\"/api/chat/sessions\")\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    assert response.json() == {\n        \"sessions\": [\n            {\n                \"session_id\": \"s-1\",\n                \"title\": \"Bellandur flood risk\",\n                \"last_active_at\": \"2026-03-10T12:00:00+00:00\",\n                \"total_turns\": 6,\n            }\n        ]\n    }\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_get_chat_session_returns_transcript(\n    client: AsyncClient, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch, auth_override: None\n) -> None:\n    now = datetime(2026, 3, 10, 12, 0, tzinfo=UTC)\n    session_row = type(\n        \"SessionRow\",\n        (),\n        {\"session_id\": \"s-1\", \"title\": \"Flood Q&A\", \"last_active_at\": now},\n    )()\n    user_msg = type(\"Msg\", (), {\"role\": \"user\", \"message\": \"Hi\", \"timestamp\": now})()\n    assistant_msg = type(\"Msg\", (), {\"role\": \"assistant\", \"message\": \"Hello\", \"timestamp\": now})()\n\n    async def _fake_list_session_messages(**_: object) -> tuple[object, list[object]]:\n        return session_row, [user_msg, assistant_msg]\n\n    monkeypatch.setattr(conversation_service, \"list_session_messages\", _fake_list_session_messages)\n\n    response = await client.get(\"/api/chat/sessions/s-1\")\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    assert response.json() == {\n        \"session_id\": \"s-1\",\n        \"title\": \"Flood Q&A\",\n        \"last_active_at\": \"2026-03-10T12:00:00+00:00\",\n        \"messages\": [\n            {\"role\": \"user\", \"message\": \"Hi\", \"timestamp\": \"2026-03-10T12:00:00+00:00\"},\n            {\"role\": \"assistant\", \"message\": \"Hello\", \"timestamp\": \"2026-03-10T12:00:00+00:00\"},\n        ],\n    }\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_get_chat_session_returns_not_found_for_missing(\n    client: AsyncClient, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch, auth_override: None\n) -> None:\n    async def _fake_list_session_messages(**_: object) -> tuple[None, list[object]]:\n        return None, []\n\n    monkeypatch.setattr(conversation_service, \"list_session_messages\", _fake_list_session_messages)\n\n    response = await client.get(\"/api/chat/sessions/missing\")\n    assert response.status_code == 404\n"
  }
}

auth_override: None\n) -> None:\n    now = datetime(2026, 3, 10, 12, 0, tzinfo=UTC)\n    session_row = type(\n        \"SessionRow\",\n        (),\n        {\"session_id\": \"s-1\", \"title\": \"Flood Q&A\", \"last_active_at\": now},\n    )()\n    user_msg = type(\"Msg\", (), {\"role\": \"user\", \"message\": \"Hi\", \"timestamp\": now})()\n    assistant_msg = type(\"Msg\", (), {\"role\": \"assistant\", \"message\": \"Hello\", \"timestamp\": now})()\n\n    async def _fake_list_session_messages(**_: object) -> tuple[object, list[object]]:\n        return session_row, [user_msg, assistant_msg]\n\n    monkeypatch.setattr(conversation_service, \"list_session_messages\", _fake_list_session_messages)\n\n    response = await client.get(\"/api/chat/sessions/s-1\")\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    assert response.json() == {\n        \"session_id\": \"s-1\",\n        \"title\": \"Flood Q&A\",\n        \"last_active_at\": \"2026-03-10T12:00:00+00:00\",\n        \"messages\": [\n            {\"role\": \"user\", \"message\": \"Hi\", \"timestamp\": \"2026-03-10T12:00:00+00:00\"},\n            {\"role\": \"assistant\", \"message\": \"Hello\", \"timestamp\": \"2026-03-10T12:00:00+00:00\"},\n        ],\n    }\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_get_chat_session_returns_not_found_for_missing(\n    client: AsyncClient, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch, auth_override: None\n) -> None:\n    async def _fake_list_session_messages(**_: object) -> tuple[None, list[object]]:\n        return None, []\n\n    monkeypatch.setattr(conversation_service, \"list_session_messages\", _fake_list_session_messages)\n\n    response = await client.get(\"/api/chat/sessions/missing\")\n    assert response.status_code == 404\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 233
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T18:32:23.243Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:32:23.243Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_IEe0SE0MkPMWAkC0U6B91dR0",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/app/services/agent_proxy.py\n@@\n from __future__ import annotations\n \n+import ast\n import json\n import re\n import time\n from typing import Any\n from uuid import uuid4\n@@\n _FENCE_RE = re.compile(r\"```(?:json)?\\s*([\\s\\S]*?)```\", re.DOTALL)\n+_LEGACY_SUMMARY_RE = re.compile(\n+    r\"^\\s*summary:\\s*(?P<summary>.+?)\\.\\s*details:\\s*(?P<details>\\{[\\s\\S]*\\})\"\n+    r\"(?:\\.\\s*health_advisory:\\s*(?P<health>.+))?\\s*$\",\n+    re.IGNORECASE,\n+)\n@@\n def _strip_fences(text: str) -> str:\n     \"\"\"Return the content inside the first markdown code fence, or the original text.\"\"\"\n     match = _FENCE_RE.search(text)\n     if match:\n         return match.group(1).strip()\n     return text\n+\n+\n+def _prettify_metric_name(key: str) -> str:\n+    return key.replace(\"_\", \" \").strip().title()\n+\n+\n+def _format_table_value(value: Any) -> str | int | float | None:\n+    if isinstance(value, (int, float, str)) or value is None:\n+        return value\n+    return str(value)\n+\n+\n+def _build_table_artifact_from_details(details: dict[str, Any], title: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:\n+    comparison = details.get(\"comparison\")\n+    month_blocks = [\n+        value\n+        for key, value in details.items()\n+        if key != \"comparison\" and isinstance(value, dict) and \"month\" in value\n+    ]\n+    if len(month_blocks) < 2:\n+        return None\n+\n+    columns = [\"Metric\"] + [\n+        str(block.get(\"month\", f\"Period {index + 1}\")) for index, block in enumerate(month_blocks)\n+    ]\n+    metric_keys: list[str] = []\n+    for block in month_blocks:\n+        for key in block.keys():\n+            if key in {\"location\", \"month\"} or key in metric_keys:\n+                continue\n+            metric_keys.append(key)\n+\n+    rows: list[list[Any]] = []\n+    for metric_key in metric_keys:\n+        row: list[Any] = [_prettify_metric_name(metric_key)]\n+        for block in month_blocks:\n+            row.append(_format_table_value(block.get(metric_key)))\n+        rows.append(row)\n+\n+    if isinstance(comparison, dict):\n+        for key, value in comparison.items():\n+            row = [_prettify_metric_name(key), _format_table_value(value)]\n+            while len(row) < len(columns):\n+                row.append(None)\n+            rows.append(row)\n+\n+    return {\n+        \"type\": \"table\",\n+        \"title\": title,\n+        \"columns\": columns,\n+        \"rows\": rows,\n+        \"description\": \"Month-over-month comparison generated from MetroSense weather summaries.\",\n+    }\n+\n+\n+def _normalise_artifact(value: Any) -> dict[str, Any] | None:\n+    if not isinstance(value, dict):\n+        return None\n+\n+    artifact_type = value.get(\"type\")\n+    title = value.get(\"title\")\n+    if not isinstance(artifact_type, str) or not isinstance(title, str):\n+        return None\n+\n+    if artifact_type == \"table\":\n+        columns = value.get(\"columns\")\n+        rows = value.get(\"rows\")\n+        if not isinstance(columns, list) or not all(isinstance(item, str) for item in columns):\n+            return None\n+        if not isinstance(rows, list):\n+            return None\n+        normalised_rows: list[list[Any]] = []\n+        for row in rows:\n+            if not isinstance(row, list):\n+                return None\n+            normalised_rows.append([_format_table_value(item) for item in row])\n+        description = value.get(\"description\")\n+        return {\n+            \"type\": \"table\",\n+            \"title\": title,\n+            \"columns\": columns,\n+            \"rows\": normalised_rows,\n+            \"description\": description if isinstance(description, str) else None,\n+            \"source\": None,\n+        }\n+\n+    if artifact_type == \"html\":\n+        source = value.get(\"source\")\n+        description = value.get(\"description\")\n+        return {\n+            \"type\": \"html\",\n+            \"title\": title,\n+            \"source\": source if isinstance(source, str) else \"\",\n+            \"description\": description if isinstance(description, str) else None,\n+            \"columns\": None,\n+            \"rows\": None,\n+        }\n+\n+    return None\n+\n+\n+def _parse_legacy_summary_details(raw_text: str, session_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:\n+    match = _LEGACY_SUMMARY_RE.match(raw_text.strip())\n+    if not match:\n+        return None\n+\n+    summary = match.group(\"summary\").strip()\n+    details_text = match.group(\"details\").strip()\n+    health_advisory = match.group(\"health\")\n+\n+    try:\n+        details = ast.literal_eval(details_text)\n+    except (ValueError, SyntaxError):\n+        return None\n+\n+    if not isinstance(details, dict):\n+        return None\n+\n+    artifact = _build_table_artifact_from_details(details, title=\"Weather Comparison\")\n+    if artifact is None:\n+        return None\n+\n+    response_text = summary\n+    if isinstance(health_advisory, str) and health_advisory.strip():\n+        response_text = f\"{summary}\\n\\nHealth note: {health_advisory.strip()}\"\n+\n+    return {\n+        \"session_id\": session_id,\n+        \"response_mode\": \"text\",\n+        \"response_text\": response_text,\n+        \"citations_summary\": [],\n+        \"data_freshness_summary\": {},\n+        \"risk_card\": None,\n+        \"artifact\": artifact,\n+        \"follow_up_prompt\": None,\n+        \"message\": response_text,\n+    }\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
```

\"rows\": normalised_rows,\n+            \"description\": description if isinstance(description, str) else None,\n+            \"source\": None,\n+        }\n+\n+    if artifact_type == \"html\":\n+        source = value.get(\"source\")\n+        description = value.get(\"description\")\n+        return {\n+            \"type\": \"html\",\n+            \"title\": title,\n+            \"source\": source if isinstance(source, str) else \"\",\n+            \"description\": description if isinstance(description, str) else None,\n+            \"columns\": None,\n+            \"rows\": None,\n+        }\n+\n+    return None\n+\n+\n+def _parse_legacy_summary_details(raw_text: str, session_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:\n+    match = _LEGACY_SUMMARY_RE.match(raw_text.strip())\n+    if not match:\n+        return None\n+\n+    summary = match.group(\"summary\").strip()\n+    details_text = match.group(\"details\").strip()\n+    health_advisory = match.group(\"health\")\n+\n+    try:\n+        details = ast.literal_eval(details_text)\n+    except (ValueError, SyntaxError):\n+        return None\n+\n+    if not isinstance(details, dict):\n+        return None\n+\n+    artifact = _build_table_artifact_from_details(details, title=\"Weather Comparison\")\n+    if artifact is None:\n+        return None\n+\n+    response_text = summary\n+    if isinstance(health_advisory, str) and health_advisory.strip():\n+        response_text = f\"{summary}\\n\\nHealth note: {health_advisory.strip()}\"\n+\n+    return {\n+        \"session_id\": session_id,\n+        \"response_mode\": \"text\",\n+        \"response_text\": response_text,\n+        \"citations_summary\": [],\n+        \"data_freshness_summary\": {},\n+        \"risk_card\": None,\n+        \"artifact\": artifact,\n+        \"follow_up_prompt\": None,\n+        \"message\": response_text,\n+    }\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
```

  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:32:35.799Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_2L3ly1dxXyoP9ADBTY5vqIsJ",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/app/services/agent_proxy.py\n@@\n def _parse_level2_payload(raw_text: str, session_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:\n     default_payload: dict[str, Any] = {\n         \"session_id\": session_id,\n         \"response_mode\": \"text\",\n@@\n     # Strip markdown code fences that LLMs sometimes emit around JSON output.\n     json_candidate = _strip_fences(raw_text)\n \n     try:\n         parsed = json.loads(json_candidate)\n     except json.JSONDecodeError:\n+        legacy_payload = _parse_legacy_summary_details(raw_text, session_id)\n+        if legacy_payload is not None:\n+            return legacy_payload\n         return default_payload\n \n     if not isinstance(parsed, dict):\n         return default_payload\n+\n+    summary = parsed.get(\"summary\")\n+    details = parsed.get(\"details\")\n+    if isinstance(summary, str) and isinstance(details, dict):\n+        artifact = _build_table_artifact_from_details(details, title=\"Weather Comparison\")\n+        if artifact is not None:\n+            health_advisory = parsed.get(\"health_advisory\")\n+            response_text = summary\n+            if isinstance(health_advisory, str) and health_advisory.strip():\n+                response_text = f\"{summary}\\n\\nHealth note: {health_advisory.strip()}\"\n+            return {\n+                \"session_id\": session_id,\n+                \"response_mode\": \"text\",\n+                \"response_text\": response_text,\n+                \"citations_summary\": _normalise_citations(parsed.get(\"citations_summary\", [])),\n+                \"data_freshness_summary\": _normalise_freshness(\n+                    parsed.get(\"data_freshness_summary\")\n+                ),\n+                \"risk_card\": _normalise_risk_card(parsed.get(\"risk_card\")),\n+                \"artifact\": artifact,\n+                \"follow_up_prompt\": parsed.get(\"follow_up_prompt\"),\n+                \"message\": response_text,\n+            }\n \n     # --- Level-2 shape: has response_text directly ---\n     response_text = parsed.get(\"response_text\")\n     if isinstance(response_text, str) and response_text.strip():\n         return {\n@@\n             \"response_mode\": parsed.get(\"response_mode\", \"text\"),\n             \"response_text\": response_text,\n             \"citations_summary\": _normalise_citations(parsed.get(\"citations_summary\", [])),\n             \"data_freshness_summary\": _normalise_freshness(parsed.get(\"data_freshness_summary\")),\n             \"risk_card\": _normalise_risk_card(parsed.get(\"risk_card\")),\n-            \"artifact\": parsed.get(\"artifact\"),\n+            \"artifact\": _normalise_artifact(parsed.get(\"artifact\")),\n             \"follow_up_prompt\": parsed.get(\"follow_up_prompt\"),\n             \"message\": response_text,\n         }\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}

    "content": [

        "type": "output_text",
        "text": "The parser now understands the leaked comparison format and can emit a native table artifact. I’m updating the prompt so the model is asked for this shape directly, then I’ll wire the frontend renderer and tests to the new `table` artifact type."
      }
    ],
    "phase": "commentary"
  }
}
```

## Entry 250
