**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:33:16.747Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_0FHkJoWkZCD858y6kRjBmgdO",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/client/src/components/chat/ArtifactRenderer.tsx\n@@\n import { useMemo, useState } from \"react\";\n import OpenInFullRoundedIcon from \"@mui/icons-material/OpenInFullRounded\";\n import WarningAmberRoundedIcon from \"@mui/icons-material/WarningAmberRounded\";\n-import { Box, Dialog, DialogContent, DialogTitle, IconButton, Skeleton, Stack, Typography } from \"@mui/material\";\n+import {\n+  Box,\n+  Dialog,\n+  DialogContent,\n+  DialogTitle,\n+  IconButton,\n+  Skeleton,\n+  Stack,\n+  Table,\n+  TableBody,\n+  TableCell,\n+  TableContainer,\n+  TableHead,\n+  TableRow,\n+  Typography,\n+} from \"@mui/material\";\n import { buildArtifactSrcDoc, sanitizeArtifactHtml } from \"@/lib/artifactSandbox\";\n import type { ArtifactPayload } from \"@/types/chat\";\n@@\n export default function ArtifactRenderer({ artifact }: ArtifactRendererProps) {\n   const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);\n \n-  const sanitized = useMemo(() => sanitizeArtifactHtml(artifact.source), [artifact.source]);\n-  const srcDoc = useMemo(() => buildArtifactSrcDoc(sanitized, artifact.title), [sanitized, artifact.title]);\n-  const hasRenderableContent = sanitized.length > 0;\n-\n-  const frame = hasRenderableContent ? (\n-    <Box\n-      component=\"iframe\"\n-      title={artifact.title}\n-      srcDoc={srcDoc}\n-      sandbox=\"\"\n-      sx={{ width: \"100%\", height: 280, border: 0, display: \"block\", backgroundColor: \"#fff\" }}\n-    />\n-  ) : (\n-    <Stack direction=\"row\" spacing={1} alignItems=\"flex-start\" sx={{ border: \"1px solid #F2C7CE\", borderRadius: 2, backgroundColor: \"#FFF0F2\", p: 2 }}>\n-      <WarningAmberRoundedIcon color=\"error\" />\n-      <Box>\n-        <Typography fontWeight={700}>Chart could not be rendered</Typography>\n-        <Typography color=\"text.secondary\">{artifact.description ?? \"The artifact did not contain safe renderable HTML.\"}</Typography>\n-      </Box>\n-    </Stack>\n-  );\n+  const sanitized = useMemo(\n+    () => (artifact.type === \"html\" ? sanitizeArtifactHtml(artifact.source) : \"\"),\n+    [artifact],\n+  );\n+  const srcDoc = useMemo(\n+    () => (artifact.type === \"html\" ? buildArtifactSrcDoc(sanitized, artifact.title) : \"\"),\n+    [artifact, sanitized],\n+  );\n+  const hasRenderableContent = artifact.type === \"html\" && sanitized.length > 0;\n+\n+  const frame =\n+    artifact.type === \"table\" ? (\n+      <TableContainer>\n+        <Table size=\"small\" aria-label={artifact.title}>\n+          <TableHead>\n+            <TableRow sx={{ backgroundColor: \"#F7F9FC\" }}>\n+              {artifact.columns.map((column) => (\n+                <TableCell key={column} sx={{ fontWeight: 700, borderBottom: \"2px solid #D1DBE8\", fontSize: 12 }}>\n+                  {column}\n+                </TableCell>\n+              ))}\n+            </TableRow>\n+          </TableHead>\n+          <TableBody>\n+            {artifact.rows.map((row, index) => (\n+              <TableRow key={`${artifact.title}-${index}`} sx={{ backgroundColor: index % 2 === 0 ? \"#FFFFFF\" : \"#F7F9FC\" }}>\n+                {row.map((cell, cellIndex) => (\n+                  <TableCell key={`${artifact.title}-${index}-${cellIndex}`} sx={{ fontSize: 13, verticalAlign: \"top\" }}>\n+                    {cell == null ? \"-\" : String(cell)}\n+                  </TableCell>\n+                ))}\n+              </TableRow>\n+            ))}\n+          </TableBody>\n+        </Table>\n+      </TableContainer>\n+    ) : hasRenderableContent ? (\n+      <Box\n+        component=\"iframe\"\n+        title={artifact.title}\n+        srcDoc={srcDoc}\n+        sandbox=\"\"\n+        sx={{ width: \"100%\", height: 280, border: 0, display: \"block\", backgroundColor: \"#fff\" }}\n+      />\n+    ) : (\n+      <Stack direction=\"row\" spacing={1} alignItems=\"flex-start\" sx={{ border: \"1px solid #F2C7CE\", borderRadius: 2, backgroundColor: \"#FFF0F2\", p: 2 }}>\n+        <WarningAmberRoundedIcon color=\"error\" />\n+        <Box>\n+          <Typography fontWeight={700}>Chart could not be rendered</Typography>\n+          <Typography color=\"text.secondary\">\n+            {artifact.description ?? \"The artifact did not contain safe renderable HTML.\"}\n+          </Typography>\n+        </Box>\n+      </Stack>\n+    );\n@@\n       <Stack direction=\"row\" alignItems=\"center\" justifyContent=\"space-between\" sx={{ px: 2, py: 1.25, backgroundColor: \"#F7F9FC\", borderBottom: \"1px solid #D1DBE8\" }}>\n         <Typography fontSize={13} fontWeight={600}>\n           {artifact.title}\n         </Typography>\n-        <IconButton aria-label={`Expand chart: ${artifact.title}`} onClick={() => setOpen(true)}>\n+        <IconButton aria-label={`Expand artifact: ${artifact.title}`} onClick={() => setOpen(true)}>\n           <OpenInFullRoundedIcon fontSize=\"small\" />\n         </IconButton>\n       </Stack>\n       <Box sx={{ p: 2, minHeight: 200 }}>\n-        {artifact.source ? frame : <Skeleton variant=\"rounded\" height={220} />}\n+        {artifact.type === \"table\" || artifact.source ? frame : <Skeleton variant=\"rounded\" height={220} />}\n       </Box>\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
```

    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/client/src/components/chat/ArtifactRenderer.tsx\n@@\n import { useMemo, useState } from \"react\";\n import OpenInFullRoundedIcon from \"@mui/icons-material/OpenInFullRounded\";\n import WarningAmberRoundedIcon from \"@mui/icons-material/WarningAmberRounded\";\n-import { Box, Dialog, DialogContent, DialogTitle, IconButton, Skeleton, Stack, Typography } from \"@mui/material\";\n+import {\n+  Box,\n+  Dialog,\n+  DialogContent,\n+  DialogTitle,\n+  IconButton,\n+  Skeleton,\n+  Stack,\n+  Table,\n+  TableBody,\n+  TableCell,\n+  TableContainer,\n+  TableHead,\n+  TableRow,\n+  Typography,\n+} from \"@mui/material\";\n import { buildArtifactSrcDoc, sanitizeArtifactHtml } from \"@/lib/artifactSandbox\";\n import type { ArtifactPayload } from \"@/types/chat\";\n@@\n export default function ArtifactRenderer({ artifact }: ArtifactRendererProps) {\n   const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);\n \n-  const sanitized = useMemo(() => sanitizeArtifactHtml(artifact.source), [artifact.source]);\n-  const srcDoc = useMemo(() => buildArtifactSrcDoc(sanitized, artifact.title), [sanitized, artifact.title]);\n-  const hasRenderableContent = sanitized.length > 0;\n-\n-  const frame = hasRenderableContent ? (\n+      <TableContainer>\n+        <Table size=\"small\" aria-label={artifact.title}>\n+          <TableHead>\n+            <TableRow sx={{ backgroundColor: \"#F7F9FC\" }}>\n+              {artifact.columns.map((column) => (\n+                <TableCell key={column} sx={{ fontWeight: 700, borderBottom: \"2px solid #D1DBE8\", fontSize: 12 }}>\n+                  {column}\n+                </TableCell>\n+              ))}\n+            </TableRow>\n+          </TableHead>\n+          <TableBody>\n+            {artifact.rows.map((row, index) => (\n+              <TableRow key={`${artifact.title}-${index}`} sx={{ backgroundColor: index % 2 === 0 ? \"#FFFFFF\" : \"#F7F9FC\" }}>\n+                {row.map((cell, cellIndex) => (\n+                  <TableCell key={`${artifact.title}-${index}-${cellIndex}`} sx={{ fontSize: 13, verticalAlign: \"top\" }}>\n+                    {cell == null ? \"-\" : String(cell)}\n+                  </TableCell>\n+                ))}\n+              </TableRow>\n+            ))}\n+          </TableBody>\n+        </Table>\n+      </TableContainer>\n+    ) : hasRenderableContent ? (\n+      <Box\n+        component=\"iframe\"\n+        title={artifact.title}\n+        srcDoc={srcDoc}\n+        sandbox=\"\"\n+        sx={{ width: \"100%\", height: 280, border: 0, display: \"block\", backgroundColor: \"#fff\" }}\n+      />\n+    ) : (\n+      <Stack direction=\"row\" spacing={1} alignItems=\"flex-start\" sx={{ border: \"1px solid #F2C7CE\", borderRadius: 2, backgroundColor: \"#FFF0F2\", p: 2 }}>\n+        <WarningAmberRoundedIcon color=\"error\" />\n+        <Box>\n+          <Typography fontWeight={700}>Chart could not be rendered</Typography>\n+          <Typography color=\"text.secondary\">\n+            {artifact.description ?? \"The artifact did not contain safe renderable HTML.\"}\n+          </Typography>\n+        </Box>\n+      </Stack>\n+    );\n@@\n       <Stack direction=\"row\" alignItems=\"center\" justifyContent=\"space-between\" sx={{ px: 2, py: 1.25, backgroundColor: \"#F7F9FC\", borderBottom: \"1px solid #D1DBE8\" }}>\n         <Typography fontSize={13} fontWeight={600}>\n           {artifact.title}\n         </Typography>\n-        <IconButton aria-label={`Expand chart: ${artifact.title}`} onClick={() => setOpen(true)}>\n+        <IconButton aria-label={`Expand artifact: ${artifact.title}`} onClick={() => setOpen(true)}>\n           <OpenInFullRoundedIcon fontSize=\"small\" />\n         </IconButton>\n       </Stack>\n       <Box sx={{ p: 2, minHeight: 200 }}>\n-        {artifact.source ? frame : <Skeleton variant=\"rounded\" height={220} />}\n+        {artifact.type === \"table\" || artifact.source ? frame : <Skeleton variant=\"rounded\" height={220} />}\n       </Box>\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 259
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T18:33:42.153Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:33:42.153Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_183aMF8MfVQWAWFlPIdIRoNl",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/tests/unit/test_chat_api.py\n@@\n async def test_chat_returns_json_payload(\n@@\n     assert response.json() == {\n@@\n         },\n     }\n+\n+\n+@pytest.mark.asyncio\n+async def test_chat_accepts_table_artifact_payload(\n+    client: AsyncClient, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch, auth_override: None\n+) -> None:\n+    async def _fake_chat_response(**_: object) -> dict[str, object]:\n+        return {\n+            \"session_id\": \"test-session\",\n+            \"response_mode\": \"text\",\n+            \"response_text\": \"Comparison ready.\",\n+            \"citations_summary\": [],\n+            \"data_freshness_summary\": {},\n+            \"follow_up_prompt\": None,\n+            \"message\": \"Comparison ready.\",\n+            \"risk_card\": None,\n+            \"artifact\": {\n+                \"type\": \"table\",\n+                \"title\": \"Bellandur Weather Comparison\",\n+                \"columns\": [\"Metric\", \"September 2025\", \"October 2025\"],\n+                \"rows\": [[\"Avg Temperature (C)\", 23.3, 24.3]],\n+                \"description\": \"Monthly comparison\",\n+            },\n+        }\n+\n+    monkeypatch.setattr(agent_proxy, \"get_chat_response\", _fake_chat_response)\n+\n+    response = await client.post(\n+        \"/api/chat\",\n+        json={\n+            \"session_id\": \"test-session\",\n+            \"message\": \"Compare Bellandur weather in September and October 2025 in a table\",\n+        },\n+    )\n+\n+    assert response.status_code == 200\n+    assert response.json()[\"artifact\"] == {\n+        \"type\": \"table\",\n+        \"title\": \"Bellandur Weather Comparison\",\n+        \"source\": None,\n+        \"columns\": [\"Metric\", \"September 2025\", \"October 2025\"],\n+        \"rows\": [[\"Avg Temperature (C)\", 23.3, 24.3]],\n+        \"description\": \"Monthly comparison\",\n+    }\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
```

  }
}

## Entry 312
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T18:34:39.712Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:34:39.712Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_message",
    "message": "Implemented end to end.\n\nThe backend now converts bad comparison output like your Bellandur example into a canonical chat response with a native table artifact instead of leaking `summary: ... details: {...}` text. It also normalizes legacy `risk_card`, `citations_summary`, and artifact shapes in [agent_proxy.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/app/services/agent_proxy.py). The chat API schema in [chat.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/app/api/routes/chat.py) now supports both `html` and `table` artifacts.\n\nThe prompt in [chat.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/prompts/chat.py) now explicitly tells the agent to return a table artifact for comparison/table requests and to never dump raw dicts or `summary/details/health_advisory` labels into `response_text`.\n\nOn the client, [chat.ts](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/client/src/types/chat.ts) now defines a discriminated artifact union, and [ArtifactRenderer.tsx](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/client/src/components/chat/ArtifactRenderer.tsx) renders table artifacts as actual tables. I added regression coverage for the legacy weather-comparison blob, the new table artifact API shape, and frontend table rendering in [test_agent_proxy.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/tests/unit/test_agent_proxy.py), [test_chat_api.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/tests/unit/test_chat_api.py), and [ArtifactRenderer.test.tsx](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/client/src/components/chat/ArtifactRenderer.test.tsx).\n\nVerification:\n- `cd server && uv run pytest tests/unit/test_agent_proxy.py tests/unit/test_chat_api.py -q`\n- `cd server && uv run ruff check app/services/agent_proxy.py app/api/routes/chat.py tests/unit/test_agent_proxy.py tests/unit/test_chat_api.py`\n- `cd client && npm run test -- --run src/components/chat/ArtifactRenderer.test.tsx src/hooks/useChat.test.tsx`\n- `cd client && npm run build`\n\nAll passed.",
    "phase": "final_answer"
  }
}
```

    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Project Identity\n- This repository is the working MetroSense codebase: `client/` for React, `server/` for FastAPI, PostgreSQL via Docker Compose, and CI/quality gates.\n- The product is `MetroSense`, a chat-based climate and infrastructure intelligence system for Bengaluru.\n- The target architecture is a three-service system:\n  - `client/`: frontend chat application\n  - `server/`: public backend API and orchestration layer\n  - `agents/`: internal agent server and tool-execution layer (✅ **exists and wired**, current status: skeleton with google_search tool only)\n- Treat the current implementation as the baseline and evolve it toward this three-service design instead of creating parallel app structures.\n- **Status**: ~70% complete. Auth flow, backend-to-agent routing, database schema, internal data routes, custom domain agent tools, structured response contract, and full subagent hierarchy are functional. Data seeding (script ready, not yet run), streaming, and E2E tests remain.\n\n## Canonical Architecture\n- The intended request flow is:\n  - `frontend -> backend -> agent`\n- The frontend should never call the agent service directly.\n- `server/` is the only public API boundary for browser clients.\n- `agents/` is an internal service boundary responsible for agent runtime behavior, tool usage, and structured chat output generation.\n- If existing docs still use `agent/` singular, treat that as referring to the planned `agents/` subsystem until naming is aligned across the repo.\n\n## Why This Boundary Exists\n- Keep secrets, database access, internal tools, and agent infrastructure off the public internet.\n- Let the backend own request validation, session coordination, auth, rate limiting, logging, and response shaping.\n- Preserve a stable frontend contract even if the agent framework, prompting strategy, or toolchain changes.\n- Keep observability and failure handling centralized in the backend rather than split between browser and agent server.\n\n## Tech Stack\n- Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic, PostgreSQL 16, Loguru, `uv`\n- Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite 5, MUI v6, React Query, Zustand, Axios, `pnpm`\n- Tooling: Ruff, MyPy strict, import-linter, ESLint, Docker, Docker Compose, GitHub Actions\n\n## Repository Shape\n- `client/`\n  - Chat UI, streaming presentation, structured response rendering, browser-facing state management.\n- `server/`\n  - Public HTTP API, request validation, orchestration, session handling, backend-to-agent coordination.\n- `agents/`\n  - Internal agent service for chat generation, tool calls, retrieval, and domain intelligence.\n- `server/app/api/`\n  - FastAPI routes and request/response boundaries.\n- `server/app/services/`\n  - Backend business logic and internal orchestration code.\n- `server/app/db/`\n  - Database models, metadata, and session management.\n- `server/tests/unit/`\n  - Fast isolated tests for backend logic and simple route behavior.\n- `server/tests/integration/`\n  - App-level and database-backed tests.\n- `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`\n  - Source of truth for the planned MetroSense chat UX and structured response behavior.\n\n## Architectural Rules\n- Preserve backend layering:\n  - `app.api` may depend on `app.services` and `app.core`\n  - `app.services` may depend on `app.db` and `app.core`\n  - `app.db` must not depend on `app.services` or `app.api`\n- Keep the frontend focused on rendering and interaction, not business orchestration.\n- Route all browser chat traffic through `server/`, even after `agents/` exists.\n- Treat backend-to-agent communication as an internal interface that may evolve without breaking the UI.\n- Prefer extending existing `server/` and `client/` entrypoints over introducing duplicate service layers or alternative public APIs.\n\n## Authentication\n- Public endpoints:\n  - `POST /api/auth/signup`\n  - `POST /api/auth/login`\n- Protected endpoints:\n  - All other `/api/*` routes require a valid JWT cookie.\n- `/health` remains public for uptime checks.\n\n## Product Direction\n- MetroSense is intended to be a chat-first application for weather risk, flooding, AQI, outage, traffic, and infrastructure-readiness questions.\n- Expected response modes:\n  - streamed assistant text for most responses\n  - agent-emitted `RiskCard` payloads for structured neighborhood risk outputs\n  - agent-emitted `Artifact` payloads for charts, maps, tables, or other visual responses\n- Structured outputs should be decided by the agent layer and delivered through the backend to the frontend.\n- The frontend must render structured responses based on explicit backend/agent event types, not keyword inference.\n\n## Testing And Validation\n- Backend checks from `server/`:\n  - `uv sync --all-extras`\n  - `uv run ruff format --check .`\n  - `uv run ruff check .`\n  - `uv run mypy .`\n  - `uv run lint-imports`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/unit -q`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/integration -q`\n- Agents checks from `agents/`:\n  - `uv sync`\n  - `uv run python scripts/smoke_test.py`\n- Frontend checks from `client/`:\n  - `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`\n  - `pnpm run lint`\n  - `pnpm run build`\n  - `pnpm exec playwright install`\n  - `pnpm run test:e2e`\n- Local startup today:\n  - The agents service runs behind a lightweight proxy that enforces the `X-Internal-Token` shared secret. The backend calls the proxy on port 8020, and the proxy forwards to the ADK server on port 8021. `/health` stays public for simple availability checks.\n  - `docker-compose up -d db`\n  - `cd server && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=your-internal-token-here && uv run alembic upgrade head && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=your-internal-token-here && uv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8021 .`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=your-internal-token-here && uv run uvicorn app.proxy:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020`\n  - `cd agents && uv run adk web --port 8001` (optional UI)\n  - `cd client && pnpm install && pnpm run dev`\n- When expanding `agents/`, keep startup and validation commands updated in this file.\n\n## Current Coverage And Next-Step Expectations\n\n### Implemented (✅)\n- **Backend:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout/me with JWT + httponly cookies), health checks, database schema (10+ models for weather/AQI/flooding/outages/traffic, sessions, conversation history), `/api/chat` endpoint with full `RiskCardPayload` and `ArtifactPayload` response schema\n- **Backend Internal Routes:** All `/internal/*` data routes (weather, AQI, lakes, floods, outages, traffic, ward profile, locations) — token-gated, consumed only by agent tools\n- **Backend Services:** `data_service.py` (all domain DB queries + neighbourhood→zone mapping), `conversation_service.py` (session upsert + turn append to `ConversationHistory`), `agent_proxy.py` (Level-2 and Level-1 ADK response parsing)\n- **Backend-to-Agent:** Proxy service with `X-Internal-Token` validation, session routing to ADK\n- **Agent Subagent Hierarchy:** Five-agent system (`root_agent → chat_agent → flood_vulnerability_agent / heat_health_agent / infrastructure_agent / logistics_agent`) using `gemini-2.5-flash`\n- **Agent Tools:** `flood_tools` (lake hydrology, flood incidents), `heat_tools` (AQI current/historical, ward profile), `infra_tools` (power outage events), `logistics_tools` (traffic current/corridor), `shared/weather_tools` (weather current/historical), `shared/location_tools` (resolve/list locations), `shared/document_tools` (list indexes, fetch section)\n- **Orchestration:** `routing.py` (keyword-based intent classification: flood/heat/infra/logistics/greeting), `response_contract.py` (`build_level2_response()` structured output builder)\n- **Prompts:** Domain-specific system prompts for all five agents; `CHAT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION` mandates JSON-only structured output with data freshness caveats and off-domain refusal\n- **Data Loader Script:** `server/scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py` — reads 6 CSVs and inserts reference + domain tables; run with `uv run python scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py --replace` (not yet executed)\n- **Frontend:** Chat UI (MessageBubble, MessageList, InputBar, ThinkingIndicator, SuggestedPrompts), auth context (login/signup/logout), RiskCard and ArtifactRenderer components, health polling\n- **Tests:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout/me), password hashing, health checks, basic chat endpoint\n\n### Planned (📋)\n- **Data Seeding:** `load_metrosense_dataset.py` exists and is ready to run; CSVs not yet loaded into PostgreSQL — run once before agent tools can return real data\n- **Document Ingestion:** Reference documents (`Documents_Metrosense/`) are read directly from the filesystem by `document_tools`; no DB ingestion needed\n- **Streaming:** Responses are buffered POST/response; SSE or WebSocket streaming not yet implemented\n- **Message Context Loading:** `ConversationHistory` model and `conversation_service` exist; conversation context not yet injected into agent session on new requests\n- **Structured Risk Card Generation:** End-to-end schema is wired (`RiskCardPayload` in routes, `build_level2_response()` in agents); requires data seeding + agent logic to populate fields\n- **E2E Tests:** Playwright configured; no test suites written yet\n- **Chat Response Tests:** Coverage for agent output parsing, risk card generation, artifact rendering\n\n### Current Test Coverage\n- Backend: Health, auth (signup/login/logout/me), password hashing, basic chat routing\n- Frontend: Component renders (no E2E tests written yet)\n- Agents: smoke_test.py validates proxy routing only (not domain logic)\n\n### Next Steps (Recommended Order)\n1. **Run data loader** — `cd server && uv run python scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py --replace` to seed CSVs into PostgreSQL so agent tools return real data\n2. **Validate agent tool responses** — Smoke-test each domain tool end-to-end (internal routes → data_service → PostgreSQL) after seeding\n3. **Wire conversation context** — Pass recent `ConversationHistory` turns to the agent session so the agent has chat memory\n4. **Validate structured output** — Confirm `build_level2_response()` + `RiskCardPayload` round-trip produces renderable frontend cards\n5. **Implement streaming** — Add SSE or WebSocket to `/api/chat` for incremental text delivery\n6. **Write E2E tests** — Playwright suites covering login → chat → risk card render\n\n## Agent Guidance\n- Before major implementation work, verify repo truth from `README.md`, `server/pyproject.toml`, `client/package.json`, and `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`.\n- Prefer `rg` for search.\n- Do not document or reference unimplemented services as if they already exist.\n- **Important:** The CSV data files (`DataSet_MetroSense/`) exist in the repo and the loader script (`server/scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py`) is implemented but **not yet run**. Seed the database before expecting agent domain tools to return real data.\n- Documents (`Documents_Metrosense/`) are served directly from the filesystem by `document_tools` — no DB load needed, but `DOCUMENTS_PATH` env must point to the correct directory.\n- The `metrosense_agent/` is the production agent; `metrosearch_agent/` is a legacy skeleton that can be removed. All five subagents and their domain tools are implemented and wired into the ADK hierarchy.\n- The `response_contract.py` `build_level2_response()` builder and all Pydantic schemas (`RiskCardPayload`, `ArtifactPayload`) are in place end-to-end — the remaining gap is data availability and agent logic populating those fields.\n- If current behavior and MetroSense direction diverge, align new work to the MetroSense direction while keeping transitions explicit and incremental.\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- google-adk-python: Expert guidance on the Google Agent Development Kit (ADK) for Python. Use this skill when the user asks about building agents, using tools, streaming, callbacks, tutorials, deployment, or advanced architecture with the Google ADK in Python. (file: /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/.agents/skills/google-adk-python/SKILL.md)\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",

      "limit": 10000

(`DataSet_MetroSense/`) exist in the repo and the loader script (`server/scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py`) is implemented but **not yet run**. Seed the database before expecting agent domain tools to return real data.\n- Documents (`Documents_Metrosense/`) are served directly from the filesystem by `document_tools` — no DB load needed, but `DOCUMENTS_PATH` env must point to the correct directory.\n- The `metrosense_agent/` is the production agent; `metrosearch_agent/` is a legacy skeleton that can be removed. All five subagents and their domain tools are implemented and wired into the ADK hierarchy.\n- The `response_contract.py` `build_level2_response()` builder and all Pydantic schemas (`RiskCardPayload`, `ArtifactPayload`) are in place end-to-end — the remaining gap is data availability and agent logic populating those fields.\n- If current behavior and MetroSense direction diverge, align new work to the MetroSense direction while keeping transitions explicit and incremental.\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- google-adk-python: Expert guidance on the Google Agent Development Kit (ADK) for Python. Use this skill when the user asks about building agents, using tools, streaming, callbacks, tutorials, deployment, or advanced architecture with the Google ADK in Python. (file: /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/.agents/skills/google-adk-python/SKILL.md)\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}
```

## Entry 328
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T18:35:54.713Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:35:54.713Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_r5pgpIV4HCtZMfcIKm57TLMD",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 3c12f3\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 962\nOutput:\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom datetime import UTC, datetime\nfrom uuid import uuid4\n\nfrom sqlalchemy import func, select\nfrom sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql import insert\nfrom sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession\n\nfrom app.db.models import ConversationHistory\nfrom app.db.models import Session as DBSession\n\n\ndef _utcnow() -> datetime:\n    return datetime.now(UTC)\n\n\ndef derive_session_title(message: str, max_length: int = 80) -> str:\n    normalized = \" \".join(message.strip().split())\n    if not normalized:\n        return \"Untitled Chat\"\n    return normalized[:max_length]\n\n\nasync def session_owner_id(session: AsyncSession, session_id: str) -> int | None:\n    stmt = select(DBSession.user_id).where(DBSession.session_id == session_id)\n    return (await session.execute(stmt)).scalar_one_or_none()\n\n\nasync def upsert_session(\n    session: AsyncSession,\n    session_id: str,\n    user_id: int,\n    title: str,\n    user_role: str = \"user\",\n) -> None:\n    now = _utcnow()\n    statement = insert(DBSession).values(\n        session_id=session_id,\n        user_id=user_id,\n        user_role=user_role,\n        title=title,\n        created_at=now,\n        last_active_at=now,\n        total_turns=1,\n        active_flag=True,\n    )\n    statement = statement.on_conflict_do_update(\n        index_elements=[DBSession.session_id],\n        set_={\n            \"user_id\": func.coalesce(DBSession.user_id, user_id),\n            \"last_active_at\": now,\n            \"total_turns\": DBSession.total_turns + 1,\n            \"active_flag\": True,\n            \"title\": func.coalesce(DBSession.title, title),\n        },\n    )\n    await session.execute(statement)\n\n\nasync def append_turn(\n    session: AsyncSession,\n    session_id: str,\n    user_message: str,\n    assistant_message: str,\n    agents_invoked: list[str],\n    latency_ms: int | None = None,\n) -> str:\n    now = _utcnow()\n    user_turn_id = str(uuid4())\n    assistant_turn_id = str(uuid4())\n    payload = [\n        ConversationHistory(\n            turn_id=user_turn_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            role=\"user\",\n            message=user_message,\n            agents_invoked=agents_invoked,\n            response_mode=None,\n            cited_documents=[],\n            cited_records=[],\n            timestamp=now,\n            latency_ms=None,\n        ),\n        ConversationHistory(\n            turn_id=assistant_turn_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            role=\"assistant\",\n            message=assistant_message,\n            agents_invoked=agents_invoked,\n            response_mode=None,\n            cited_documents=[],\n            cited_records=[],\n            timestamp=now,\n            latency_ms=latency_ms,\n        ),\n    ]\n    session.add_all(payload)\n    return assistant_turn_id\n\n\nasync def list_sessions(\n    session: AsyncSession,\n    user_id: int,\n    limit: int = 20,\n    offset: int = 0,\n) -> list[DBSession]:\n    stmt = (\n        select(DBSession)\n        .where(DBSession.user_id == user_id)\n        .order_by(DBSession.last_active_at.desc())\n        .limit(limit)\n        .offset(offset)\n    )\n    return (await session.execute(stmt)).scalars().all()\n\n\nasync def list_session_messages(\n    session: AsyncSession,\n    user_id: int,\n    session_id: str,\n) -> tuple[DBSession | None, list[ConversationHistory]]:\n    session_stmt = select(DBSession).where(\n        DBSession.session_id == session_id, DBSession.user_id == user_id\n    )\n    owned_session = (await session.execute(session_stmt)).scalar_one_or_none()\n    if owned_session is None:\n        return None, []\n\n    history_stmt = (\n        select(ConversationHistory)\n        .where(ConversationHistory.session_id == session_id)\n        .order_by(ConversationHistory.timestamp.asc())\n    )\n    messages = (await session.execute(history_stmt)).scalars().all()\n    return owned_session, messages\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 329
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T18:35:54.718Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:35:54.718Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_stvb8UhnKRfsfJeMGjKGqpUV",
    "output": "Chunk ID: d23092\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 1505\nOutput:\n\n    card = {\n        \"neighborhood\": value.get(\"neighborhood\") or value.get(\"location\") or \"Unknown\",\n        \"generated_at\": value.get(\"generated_at\") or value.get(\"as_of\") or \"\",\n        \"overall_risk_score\": overall_risk_score,\n        \"flood_risk\": flood_risk,\n        \"power_outage_risk\": power_outage_risk,\n        \"traffic_delay_index\": traffic_delay_index,\n        \"health_advisory\": health_advisory,\n        \"emergency_readiness\": emergency_readiness,\n        \"rainfall_expected_mm_per_hr\": (\n            float(rainfall_expected) if rainfall_expected is not None else None\n        ),\n        \"rainfall_classification\": rainfall_classification,\n        \"barricade_recommendations\": barricades or None,\n    }\n    return card\n\n\ndef _strip_fences(text: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Return the content inside the first markdown code fence, or the original text.\"\"\"\n    match = _FENCE_RE.search(text)\n    if match:\n        return match.group(1).strip()\n    return text\n\n\ndef _prettify_metric_name(key: str) -> str:\n    return key.replace(\"_\", \" \").strip().title()\n\n\ndef _format_table_value(value: Any) -> str | int | float | None:\n    if isinstance(value, (int, float, str)) or value is None:\n        return value\n    return str(value)\n\n\ndef _build_table_artifact_from_details(\n    details: dict[str, Any], title: str\n) -> dict[str, Any] | None:\n    comparison = details.get(\"comparison\")\n    month_blocks = [\n        value\n        for key, value in details.items()\n        if key != \"comparison\" and isinstance(value, dict) and \"month\" in value\n    ]\n    if len(month_blocks) < 2:\n        return None\n\n    columns = [\"Metric\"] + [\n        str(block.get(\"month\", f\"Period {index + 1}\")) for index, block in enumerate(month_blocks)\n    ]\n    metric_keys: list[str] = []\n    for block in month_blocks:\n        for key in block.keys():\n            if key in {\"location\", \"month\"} or key in metric_keys:\n                continue\n            metric_keys.append(key)\n\n    rows: list[list[Any]] = []\n    for metric_key in metric_keys:\n        row: list[Any] = [_prettify_metric_name(metric_key)]\n        for block in month_blocks:\n            row.append(_format_table_value(block.get(metric_key)))\n        rows.append(row)\n\n    if isinstance(comparison, dict):\n        for key, value in comparison.items():\n            row = [_prettify_metric_name(key), _format_table_value(value)]\n            while len(row) < len(columns):\n                row.append(None)\n            rows.append(row)\n\n    return {\n        \"type\": \"table\",\n        \"title\": title,\n        \"columns\": columns,\n        \"rows\": rows,\n        \"description\": \"Month-over-month comparison generated from MetroSense weather summaries.\",\n    }\n\n\ndef _normalise_artifact(value: Any) -> dict[str, Any] | None:\n    if not isinstance(value, dict):\n        return None\n\n    artifact_type = value.get(\"type\")\n    title = value.get(\"title\")\n    if not isinstance(artifact_type, str) or not isinstance(title, str):\n        return None\n\n    if artifact_type == \"table\":\n        columns = value.get(\"columns\")\n        rows = value.get(\"rows\")\n        if not isinstance(columns, list) or not all(isinstance(item, str) for item in columns):\n            return None\n        if not isinstance(rows, list):\n            return None\n        normalised_rows: list[list[Any]] = []\n        for row in rows:\n            if not isinstance(row, list):\n                return None\n            normalised_rows.append([_format_table_value(item) for item in row])\n        description = value.get(\"description\")\n        return {\n            \"type\": \"table\",\n            \"title\": title,\n            \"columns\": columns,\n            \"rows\": normalised_rows,\n            \"description\": description if isinstance(description, str) else None,\n            \"source\": None,\n        }\n\n    if artifact_type == \"html\":\n        source = value.get(\"source\")\n        description = value.get(\"description\")\n        return {\n            \"type\": \"html\",\n            \"title\": title,\n            \"source\": source if isinstance(source, str) else \"\",\n            \"description\": description if isinstance(description, str) else None,\n            \"columns\": None,\n            \"rows\": None,\n        }\n\n    return None\n\n\ndef _parse_legacy_summary_details(raw_text: str, session_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:\n    match = _LEGACY_SUMMARY_RE.match(raw_text.strip())\n    if not match:\n        return None\n\n    summary = match.group(\"summary\").strip()\n    details_text = match.group(\"details\").strip()\n    health_advisory = match.group(\"health\")\n\n    try:\n        details = ast.literal_eval(details_text)\n    except (ValueError, SyntaxError):\n        return None\n\n    if not isinstance(details, dict):\n        return None\n\n    artifact = _build_table_artifact_from_details(details, title=\"Weather Comparison\")\n    if artifact is None:\n        return None\n\n    response_text = summary\n    if isinstance(health_advisory, str) and health_advisory.strip():\n        response_text = f\"{summary}\\n\\nHealth note: {health_advisory.strip()}\"\n\n    return {\n        \"session_id\": session_id,\n        \"response_mode\": \"text\",\n        \"response_text\": response_text,\n        \"citations_summary\": [],\n        \"data_freshness_summary\": {},\n        \"risk_card\": None,\n        \"artifact\": artifact,\n        \"follow_up_prompt\": None,\n        \"message\": response_text,\n    }\n\n\ndef _augment_message_with_runtime_context(message: str, runtime_context: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n    return (\n        \"[MetroSense Runtime Context]\\n\"\n        \"This block is injected by the backend. Use it only for availability and freshness \"\n        \"context. Tool outputs remain authoritative.\\n\"\n        f\"{json.dumps(runtime_context, separators=(',', ':'), ensure_ascii=True)}\\n\\n\"\n        \"[User Message]\\n\"\n        f\"{message}\"\n    )\n\n\ndef _synthesize_from_level1(parsed: dict[str, Any]) -> str | None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Try to build a human-readable response_text from a Level-1 agent payload\n    (fields: agent, status, confidence, data, errors, citations, …).\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 333
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T18:36:00.196Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:36:00.196Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_message",
    "message": "I found the failing path. `append_turn()` only `add_all()`s the conversation rows; then `agent_proxy` adds `AuditLog` and commits once at the end. With asyncpg/Postgres, SQLAlchemy can issue the `audit_log` insert before `conversation_history` unless we flush first, which makes the FK fail even though both are in the same transaction. I’m fixing that by flushing after the conversation rows are added, then I’ll add a regression test around audit-log persistence.",
    "phase": "commentary"
  }
}
```
**Metadata:**
```json

    "type": "agent_message",
    "message": "I found the failing path. `append_turn()` only `add_all()`s the conversation rows; then `agent_proxy` adds `AuditLog` and commits once at the end. With asyncpg/Postgres, SQLAlchemy can issue the `audit_log` insert before `conversation_history` unless we flush first, which makes the FK fail even though both are in the same transaction. I’m fixing that by flushing after the conversation rows are added, then I’ll add a regression test around audit-log persistence.",
    "phase": "commentary"
  }
}
```

## Entry 334

{

  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "message",
    "role": "assistant",
    "content": [
      {
        "type": "output_text",
        "text": "I found the failing path. `append_turn()` only `add_all()`s the conversation rows; then `agent_proxy` adds `AuditLog` and commits once at the end. With asyncpg/Postgres, SQLAlchemy can issue the `audit_log` insert before `conversation_history` unless we flush first, which makes the FK fail even though both are in the same transaction. I’m fixing that by flushing after the conversation rows are added, then I’ll add a regression test around audit-log persistence."
      }
    ],
    "phase": "commentary"
  }
}
```

      {
        "type": "output_text",
        "text": "I found the failing path. `append_turn()` only `add_all()`s the conversation rows; then `agent_proxy` adds `AuditLog` and commits once at the end. With asyncpg/Postgres, SQLAlchemy can issue the `audit_log` insert before `conversation_history` unless we flush first, which makes the FK fail even though both are in the same transaction. I’m fixing that by flushing after the conversation rows are added, then I’ll add a regression test around audit-log persistence."
      }
    ],
    "phase": "commentary"
  }
}
```
---

started_at) * 1000)\n\n    try:\n        await conversation_service.upsert_session(\n            db_session,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            user_id=user_id,\n            title=conversation_service.derive_session_title(message),\n        )\n        assistant_turn_id = await conversation_service.append_turn(\n            db_session,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            user_message=message,\n            assistant_message=response_payload[\"response_text\"],\n            agents_invoked=[],\n            latency_ms=latency_ms,\n        )\n        db_session.add(\n            AuditLog(\n                log_id=str(uuid4()),\n                session_id=session_id,\n                turn_id=assistant_turn_id,\n                query_text=message,\n                intent_classified=response_payload.get(\"response_mode\"),\n                agents_invoked=[],\n                overall_confidence=None,\n                data_freshness_lag_seconds=None,\n                stale_sources=[],\n                error_flag=False,\n                error_detail=None,\n            )\n        )\n        await db_session.commit()\n    except Exception:\n        await db_session.rollback()\n        logger.exception(\"Failed to persist conversation history for session_id={}\", session_id)\n\n    return response_payload\n\n\nasync def get_agent_health(settings: Settings) -> dict[str, str]:\n    headers = _auth_headers(settings)\n    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=5.0) as client:\n        response = await client.get(\n            f\"{settings.agent_server_url.rstrip('/')}/health\",\n            headers=headers,\n        )\n        response.raise_for_status()\n\n    return {\"backend\": \"ok\", \"agent\": \"ok\", \"status\": \"online\"}\n"

## Entry 341
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T18:36:08.073Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:36:08.073Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_message",
    "message": "The fix belongs in the conversation persistence path, not in the API route. I’m making `append_turn()` flush after inserting the conversation rows so the returned `assistant_turn_id` is guaranteed to exist before `AuditLog` references it. Then I’ll lock it down with a unit test that asserts the flush happens before commit.",
    "phase": "commentary"
  }
}
```

        "type": "output_text",
        "text": "The fix belongs in the conversation persistence path, not in the API route. I’m making `append_turn()` flush after inserting the conversation rows so the returned `assistant_turn_id` is guaranteed to exist before `AuditLog` references it. Then I’ll lock it down with a unit test that asserts the flush happens before commit."
      }
    ],
    "phase": "commentary"
  }
}
```

## Entry 343
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T18:36:10.024Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:36:10.024Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_sx3RXYxvZfK94v3IX5U6R7ZD",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/app/services/conversation_service.py\n@@\n async def append_turn(\n     session: AsyncSession,\n     session_id: str,\n     user_message: str,\n@@\n     ]\n     session.add_all(payload)\n+    # Flush so downstream rows (for example audit_log) can safely FK the assistant turn.\n+    await session.flush()\n     return assistant_turn_id\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 346
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T18:36:16.472Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:36:16.472Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_IaYRSx0b5DRUYuCCjqXm3cpv",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/tests/unit/test_agent_proxy.py\n@@\n async def test_get_chat_response_persists_and_commits(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:\n@@\n     upsert.assert_awaited_once()\n     append.assert_awaited_once()\n     db_session.commit.assert_awaited_once()\n     db_session.rollback.assert_not_awaited()\n+\n+\n+@pytest.mark.asyncio\n+async def test_append_turn_flushes_before_audit_log_fk_is_used() -> None:\n+    db_session = AsyncMock()\n+\n+    turn_id = await agent_proxy.conversation_service.append_turn(\n+        session=db_session,\n+        session_id=\"s-1\",\n+        user_message=\"hello\",\n+        assistant_message=\"world\",\n+        agents_invoked=[],\n+        latency_ms=10,\n+    )\n+\n+    assert isinstance(turn_id, str)\n+    db_session.add_all.assert_called_once()\n+    db_session.flush.assert_awaited_once()\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 372
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T18:36:48.248Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:36:48.248Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_message",
    "message": "The issue was a write-order bug in persistence.\n\n`audit_log.turn_id` has a foreign key to `conversation_history.turn_id`, but [append_turn](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/app/services/conversation_service.py) was only doing `session.add_all(...)` and returning the assistant turn id. Then [agent_proxy.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/app/services/agent_proxy.py) inserted `AuditLog` with that id in the same transaction. Because the conversation rows had not been flushed yet, Postgres could see the `audit_log` insert before the referenced `conversation_history` row existed, so the FK failed.\n\nI fixed it by flushing immediately after adding the conversation rows in [conversation_service.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/app/services/conversation_service.py). That guarantees the assistant turn exists before `AuditLog` references it. I also added a regression test in [test_agent_proxy.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/tests/unit/test_agent_proxy.py) to assert `append_turn()` flushes before commit-time audit logging.\n\nVerified with:\n```bash\ncd server\nuv run pytest tests/unit/test_agent_proxy.py tests/unit/test_chat_api.py -q\nuv run ruff check app/services/conversation_service.py tests/unit/test_agent_proxy.py\n```\nResult: `16 passed`.",
    "phase": "final_answer"
  }
}
```
**Metadata:**
```json

Then [agent_proxy.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/app/services/agent_proxy.py) inserted `AuditLog` with that id in the same transaction. Because the conversation rows had not been flushed yet, Postgres could see the `audit_log` insert before the referenced `conversation_history` row existed, so the FK failed.\n\nI fixed it by flushing immediately after adding the conversation rows in [conversation_service.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/app/services/conversation_service.py). That guarantees the assistant turn exists before `AuditLog` references it. I also added a regression test in [test_agent_proxy.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/tests/unit/test_agent_proxy.py) to assert `append_turn()` flushes before commit-time audit logging.\n\nVerified with:\n```bash\ncd server\nuv run pytest tests/unit/test_agent_proxy.py tests/unit/test_chat_api.py -q\nuv run ruff check app/services/conversation_service.py tests/unit/test_agent_proxy.py\n```\nResult: `16 passed`."

  }
}

## Entry 377
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T18:37:58.182Z
**Role/Type:** turn_context
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:37:58.182Z",
  "type": "turn_context",
  "payload": {
    "turn_id": "019cde30-fc21-73a0-ba78-b18f13d889b8",
    "cwd": "/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense",
    "current_date": "2026-03-12",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "writable_roots": [
        "/home/dell/.codex/memories"
      ],
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.4",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "default",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.4",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\n\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\n\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\n\n## request_user_input availability\n\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\n\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\n"
      }
    },
    "realtime_active": false,
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "none",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Project Identity\n- This repository is the working MetroSense codebase: `client/` for React, `server/` for FastAPI, PostgreSQL via Docker Compose, and CI/quality gates.\n- The product is `MetroSense`, a chat-based climate and infrastructure intelligence system for Bengaluru.\n- The target architecture is a three-service system:\n  - `client/`: frontend chat application\n  - `server/`: public backend API and orchestration layer\n  - `agents/`: internal agent server and tool-execution layer (✅ **exists and wired**, current status: skeleton with google_search tool only)\n- Treat the current implementation as the baseline and evolve it toward this three-service design instead of creating parallel app structures.\n- **Status**: ~70% complete. Auth flow, backend-to-agent routing, database schema, internal data routes, custom domain agent tools, structured response contract, and full subagent hierarchy are functional. Data seeding (script ready, not yet run), streaming, and E2E tests remain.\n\n## Canonical Architecture\n- The intended request flow is:\n  - `frontend -> backend -> agent`\n- The frontend should never call the agent service directly.\n- `server/` is the only public API boundary for browser clients.\n- `agents/` is an internal service boundary responsible for agent runtime behavior, tool usage, and structured chat output generation.\n- If existing docs still use `agent/` singular, treat that as referring to the planned `agents/` subsystem until naming is aligned across the repo.\n\n## Why This Boundary Exists\n- Keep secrets, database access, internal tools, and agent infrastructure off the public internet.\n- Let the backend own request validation, session coordination, auth, rate limiting, logging, and response shaping.\n- Preserve a stable frontend contract even if the agent framework, prompting strategy, or toolchain changes.\n- Keep observability and failure handling centralized in the backend rather than split between browser and agent server.\n\n## Tech Stack\n- Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic, PostgreSQL 16, Loguru, `uv`\n- Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite 5, MUI v6, React Query, Zustand, Axios, `pnpm`\n- Tooling: Ruff, MyPy strict, import-linter, ESLint, Docker, Docker Compose, GitHub Actions\n\n## Repository Shape\n- `client/`\n  - Chat UI, streaming presentation, structured response rendering, browser-facing state management.\n- `server/`\n  - Public HTTP API, request validation, orchestration, session handling, backend-to-agent coordination.\n- `agents/`\n  - Internal agent service for chat generation, tool calls, retrieval, and domain intelligence.\n- `server/app/api/`\n  - FastAPI routes and request/response boundaries.\n- `server/app/services/`\n  - Backend business logic and internal orchestration code.\n- `server/app/db/`\n  - Database models, metadata, and session management.\n- `server/tests/unit/`\n  - Fast isolated tests for backend logic and simple route behavior.\n- `server/tests/integration/`\n  - App-level and database-backed tests.\n- `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`\n  - Source of truth for the planned MetroSense chat UX and structured response behavior.\n\n## Architectural Rules\n- Preserve backend layering:\n  - `app.api` may depend on `app.services` and `app.core`\n  - `app.services` may depend on `app.db` and `app.core`\n  - `app.db` must not depend on `app.services` or `app.api`\n- Keep the frontend focused on rendering and interaction, not business orchestration.\n- Route all browser chat traffic through `server/`, even after `agents/` exists.\n- Treat backend-to-agent communication as an internal interface that may evolve without breaking the UI.\n- Prefer extending existing `server/` and `client/` entrypoints over introducing duplicate service layers or alternative public APIs.\n\n## Authentication\n- Public endpoints:\n  - `POST /api/auth/signup`\n  - `POST /api/auth/login`\n- Protected endpoints:\n  - All other `/api/*` routes require a valid JWT cookie.\n- `/health` remains public for uptime checks.\n\n## Product Direction\n- MetroSense is intended to be a chat-first application for weather risk, flooding, AQI, outage, traffic, and infrastructure-readiness questions.\n- Expected response modes:\n  - streamed assistant text for most responses\n  - agent-emitted `RiskCard` payloads for structured neighborhood risk outputs\n  - agent-emitted `Artifact` payloads for charts, maps, tables, or other visual responses\n- Structured outputs should be decided by the agent layer and delivered through the backend to the frontend.\n- The frontend must render structured responses based on explicit backend/agent event types, not keyword inference.\n\n## Testing And Validation\n- Backend checks from `server/`:\n  - `uv sync --all-extras`\n  - `uv run ruff format --check .`\n  - `uv run ruff check .`\n  - `uv run mypy .`\n  - `uv run lint-imports`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/unit -q`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/integration -q`\n- Agents checks from `agents/`:\n  - `uv sync`\n  - `uv run python scripts/smoke_test.py`\n- Frontend checks from `client/`:\n  - `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`\n  - `pnpm run lint`\n  - `pnpm run build`\n  - `pnpm exec playwright install`\n  - `pnpm run test:e2e`\n- Local startup today:\n  - The agents service runs behind a lightweight proxy that enforces the `X-Internal-Token` shared secret. The backend calls the proxy on port 8020, and the proxy forwards to the ADK server on port 8021. `/health` stays public for simple availability checks.\n  - `docker-compose up -d db`\n  - `cd server && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=your-internal-token-here && uv run alembic upgrade head && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=your-internal-token-here && uv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8021 .`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=your-internal-token-here && uv run uvicorn app.proxy:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020`\n  - `cd agents && uv run adk web --port 8001` (optional UI)\n  - `cd client && pnpm install && pnpm run dev`\n- When expanding `agents/`, keep startup and validation commands updated in this file.\n\n## Current Coverage And Next-Step Expectations\n\n### Implemented (✅)\n- **Backend:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout/me with JWT + httponly cookies), health checks, database schema (10+ models for weather/AQI/flooding/outages/traffic, sessions, conversation history), `/api/chat` endpoint with full `RiskCardPayload` and `ArtifactPayload` response schema\n- **Backend Internal Routes:** All `/internal/*` data routes (weather, AQI, lakes, floods, outages, traffic, ward profile, locations) — token-gated, consumed only by agent tools\n- **Backend Services:** `data_service.py` (all domain DB queries + neighbourhood→zone mapping), `conversation_service.py` (session upsert + turn append to `ConversationHistory`), `agent_proxy.py` (Level-2 and Level-1 ADK response parsing)\n- **Backend-to-Agent:** Proxy service with `X-Internal-Token` validation, session routing to ADK\n- **Agent Subagent Hierarchy:** Five-agent system (`root_agent → chat_agent → flood_vulnerability_agent / heat_health_agent / infrastructure_agent / logistics_agent`) using `gemini-2.5-flash`\n- **Agent Tools:** `flood_tools` (lake hydrology, flood incidents), `heat_tools` (AQI current/historical, ward profile), `infra_tools` (power outage events), `logistics_tools` (traffic current/corridor), `shared/weather_tools` (weather current/historical), `shared/location_tools` (resolve/list locations), `shared/document_tools` (list indexes, fetch section)\n- **Orchestration:** `routing.py` (keyword-based intent classification: flood/heat/infra/logistics/greeting), `response_contract.py` (`build_level2_response()` structured output builder)\n- **Prompts:** Domain-specific system prompts for all five agents; `CHAT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION` mandates JSON-only structured output with data freshness caveats and off-domain refusal\n- **Data Loader Script:** `server/scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py` — reads 6 CSVs and inserts reference + domain tables; run with `uv run python scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py --replace` (not yet executed)\n- **Frontend:** Chat UI (MessageBubble, MessageList, InputBar, ThinkingIndicator, SuggestedPrompts), auth context (login/signup/logout), RiskCard and ArtifactRenderer components, health polling\n- **Tests:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout/me), password hashing, health checks, basic chat endpoint\n\n### Planned (📋)\n- **Data Seeding:** `load_metrosense_dataset.py` exists and is ready to run; CSVs not yet loaded into PostgreSQL — run once before agent tools can return real data\n- **Document Ingestion:** Reference documents (`Documents_Metrosense/`) are read directly from the filesystem by `document_tools`; no DB ingestion needed\n- **Streaming:** Responses are buffered POST/response; SSE or WebSocket streaming not yet implemented\n- **Message Context Loading:** `ConversationHistory` model and `conversation_service` exist; conversation context not yet injected into agent session on new requests\n- **Structured Risk Card Generation:** End-to-end schema is wired (`RiskCardPayload` in routes, `build_level2_response()` in agents); requires data seeding + agent logic to populate fields\n- **E2E Tests:** Playwright configured; no test suites written yet\n- **Chat Response Tests:** Coverage for agent output parsing, risk card generation, artifact rendering\n\n### Current Test Coverage\n- Backend: Health, auth (signup/login/logout/me), password hashing, basic chat routing\n- Frontend: Component renders (no E2E tests written yet)\n- Agents: smoke_test.py validates proxy routing only (not domain logic)\n\n### Next Steps (Recommended Order)\n1. **Run data loader** — `cd server && uv run python scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py after seeding\n3. **Wire conversation context** — Pass recent `ConversationHistory` turns to the agent session so the agent has chat memory\n4. **Validate structured output** — Confirm E2E tests** — Playwright suites covering login → chat → risk card render\n\n## Agent Guidance\n- Before major implementation work, verify repo truth from `README.md`, `server/pyproject.toml`, `client/package.json`, and `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`.\n- Prefer `rg` for search.\n- Do not document or reference unimplemented services as if they already exist.\n- **Important:** The CSV data files Seed the database before expecting agent domain tools to return real data.\n- Documents (`Documents_Metrosense/`) are served directly from the filesystem by `document_tools` — no DB load needed, but `DOCUMENTS_PATH` env must point to the correct directory.\n- The `metrosense_agent/` is the production agent; `metrosearch_agent/` is a legacy skeleton that can be removed.     "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Project Identity\n- This repository is the working MetroSense codebase: `client/` for React, `server/` for FastAPI, PostgreSQL via Docker Compose, and CI/quality gates.\n- The product is `MetroSense`, a chat-based climate and infrastructure intelligence system for Bengaluru.\n- The target architecture is a three-service system:\n  - `client/`: frontend chat application\n  - `server/`: public backend API and orchestration layer\n  - `agents/`: internal agent server and tool-execution layer (✅ **exists and wired**, current status: skeleton with google_search tool only)\n- Treat the current implementation as the baseline and evolve it toward this three-service design instead of creating parallel app structures.\n- **Status**: ~70% complete. Auth flow, backend-to-agent routing, database schema, internal data routes, custom domain agent tools, structured response contract, and full subagent hierarchy are functional. Data seeding (script ready, not yet run), streaming, and E2E tests remain.\n\n## Canonical Architecture\n- The intended request flow is:\n  - `frontend -> backend -> agent`\n- The frontend should never call the agent service directly.\n- `server/` is the only public API boundary for browser clients.\n- `agents/` is an internal service boundary responsible for agent runtime behavior, tool usage, and structured chat output generation.\n- If existing docs still use `agent/` singular, treat that as referring to the planned `agents/` subsystem until naming is aligned across the repo.\n\n## Why This Boundary Exists\n- Keep secrets, database access, internal tools, and agent infrastructure off the public internet.\n- Let the backend own request validation, session coordination, auth, rate limiting, logging, and response shaping.\n- Preserve a stable frontend contract even if the agent framework, prompting strategy, or toolchain changes.\n- Keep observability and failure handling centralized in the backend rather than split between browser and agent server.\n\n## Tech Stack\n- Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic, PostgreSQL 16, Loguru, `uv`\n- Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite 5, MUI v6, React Query, Zustand, Axios, `pnpm`\n- Tooling: Ruff, MyPy strict, import-linter, ESLint, Docker, Docker Compose, GitHub Actions\n\n## Repository Shape\n- `client/`\n  - Chat UI, streaming presentation, structured response rendering, browser-facing state management.\n- `server/`\n  - Public HTTP API, request validation, orchestration, session handling, backend-to-agent coordination.\n- `agents/`\n  - Internal agent service for chat generation, tool calls, retrieval, and domain intelligence.\n- `server/app/api/`\n  - FastAPI routes and request/response boundaries.\n- `server/app/services/`\n  - Backend business logic and internal orchestration code.\n- `server/app/db/`\n  - Database models, metadata, and session management.\n- `server/tests/unit/`\n  - Fast isolated tests for backend logic and simple route behavior.\n- `server/tests/integration/`\n  - App-level and database-backed tests.\n- `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`\n  - Source of truth for the planned MetroSense chat UX and structured response behavior.\n\n## Architectural Rules\n- Preserve backend layering:\n  - `app.api` may depend on `app.services` and `app.core`\n  - `app.services` may depend on `app.db` and `app.core`\n  - `app.db` must not depend on `app.services` or `app.api`\n- Keep the frontend focused on rendering and interaction, not business orchestration.\n- Route all browser chat traffic through `server/`, even after `agents/` exists.\n- Treat backend-to-agent communication as an internal interface that may evolve without breaking the UI.\n- Prefer extending existing `server/` and `client/` entrypoints over introducing duplicate service layers or alternative public APIs.\n\n## Authentication\n- Public endpoints:\n  - `POST /api/auth/signup`\n  - `POST /api/auth/login`\n- Protected endpoints:\n  - All other `/api/*` routes require a valid JWT cookie.\n- `/health` remains public for uptime checks.\n\n## Product Direction\n- MetroSense is intended to be a chat-first application for weather risk, flooding, AQI, outage, traffic, and infrastructure-readiness questions.\n- Expected response modes:\n  - streamed assistant text for most responses\n  - agent-emitted `RiskCard` payloads for structured neighborhood risk outputs\n  - agent-emitted `Artifact` payloads for charts, maps, tables, or other visual responses\n- Structured outputs should be decided by the agent layer and delivered through the backend to the frontend.\n- The frontend must render structured responses based on explicit backend/agent event types, not keyword inference.\n\n## Testing And Validation\n- Backend checks from `server/`:\n  - `uv sync --all-extras`\n  - `uv run ruff format --check .`\n  - `uv run ruff check .`\n  - `uv run mypy .`\n  - `uv run lint-imports`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/unit -q`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/integration -q`\n- Agents checks from `agents/`:\n  - `uv sync`\n  - `uv run python scripts/smoke_test.py`\n- Frontend checks from `client/`:\n  - `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`\n  - `pnpm run lint`\n  - `pnpm run build`\n  - `pnpm exec playwright install`\n  - `pnpm run test:e2e`\n- Local startup today:\n  - The agents service runs behind a lightweight proxy that enforces the `X-Internal-Token` shared secret.     "phase": "commentary"
  }
}

## Entry 398
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T18:38:18.534Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T18:38:18.534Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_message",
    "message": "The crash was caused by a malformed f-string in [chat.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/prompts/chat.py).\n\n`CHAT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION` is an f-string, so literal JSON braces inside it must be escaped as `{{` and `}}`. The `citations_summary` example still had:\n```python\n[{\"source\": \"<document or tool source>\"}, \"...\"]\n```\nwhich Python tried to parse as an f-string expression during module import.\n\nI fixed it to:\n```python\n[{{\"source\": \"<document or tool source>\"}}, \"...\"]\n```\n\nVerification:\n```bash\ncd agents\nuv run python -c \"import metrosense_agent; print('import ok')\"\n```\nResult: `import ok`\n\nYou should be able to restart the ADK server now.",
    "phase": "final_answer"
  }
}
```
